Pets.Name: Princess Bloodshed
Pets.Description: Taking after her mother, Dame Bloodshed, the Princess is armed with the sharpest of claws and the furriest of ears.

Pets.Name: Voldy
Pets.Description: Sometimes the void's call is faint, so smaller, younger voidwalkers heed its call.

Pets.Name: Blitzcreek
Pets.Description: Evolving in the Voidstorm has really made its smaller inhabitants significantly more aggressive.

Pets.Name: Dragonhawk Munchkin
Pets.Description: This tiny breed of dragonhawk is popular for those who prefer to keep their companions travel sized. Just keep them away from curtains.

Pets.Name: Zo'ya
Pets.Description: Zo'ya is cunning and beautiful, both highly desirable traits among purrkins. Despite being the culprit behind many mishaps, her warm glow ensures she is forgiven for any transgression.

Pets.Name: Cerulean Lapbug
Pets.Description: Widely prized as pets, cerulean lapbugs were thought to bring good fortune.

Pets.Name: Copper Lapbug
Pets.Description: Lapbugs were widely prized as domesticated pets before the fall of K'aresh. Copper lapbugs were especially sought-after.

Pets.Name: Inquisitive Cosmic Ray Pup
Pets.Description: This cosmic ray pup is curious about a great many things. Don't let it get into trouble (again)!

Pets.Name: Sao'rhon
Pets.Description: A very friendly cosmic being. Direct eye contact will obliterate your soul.

Pets.Name: Graceful Cosmic Ray Pup
Pets.Description: Efforts to rewire the more desctructive tendencies of the species left this specimen more docile than most, but it was still not a successful endeavor.

Pets.Name: Shimmering Inchshifter
Pets.Description: Projections showed a less than one percent chance of successful integration, but this one is too cute to eliminate.

Pets.Name: Vitriolic Inchshifter
Pets.Description: Who knew these things could eat so much?

Pets.Name: Cyan Siphonmite
Pets.Description: How this siphonmite snuck its way into a stasis container is anyone's guess.

Pets.Name: Xanthous Siphonmite
Pets.Description: Siphonmites generally feed on the energies of dying worlds, but this one is perfectly content with getting some scritches from researchers instead.

Pets.Name: Impartial Watcher
Pets.Description: This watcher's ennui is as eternal as the struggle between the Light and the Void.

Pets.Name: Dread Horrorling
Pets.Description: Early attempts to integrate with the ecosystem resulted in multiple specimens being eyeblasted.

Pets.Name: Scrappy Thorntail
Pets.Description: This little thorntail just loves to get into scraps and scrapes with its greatest foe: lapbugs.

Pets.Name: Veridian Thorntail
Pets.Description: This baby thorntail's favorite activity is splashing in small puddles.

Pets.Name: Craboom
Pets.Description: Small, snappy, and with a temper as explosive as its name. Handle with caution!

Pets.Name: Void-Scarred Tallstrider Chick
Pets.Description: Rarely raised in captivity, Tallstrider's are extremely territorial. A small clutch of eggs was found during a hunt, and a tauren trainer came to Orgrimmar to show them off. However, after the fall, they have become deadly once more. So far, no one has been able to direct their corrupted forms.

Pets.Name: Void-Scarred Scorpid
Pets.Description: A creature prized by rogues for its venom. Many lived in the many holes and warrens of the Drag, but after the fall these new horrors emerged to menace the survivors.

Pets.Name: Void-Scarred Parrot
Pets.Description: Stormwind's Trade District sees many goods and animals pass through its auction house. Once the parrots of Stranglethorn were an expensive luxury. Now those present when N'zoth took over are a scourge.

Pets.Name: Eye of Chaos
Pets.Description: For so many years Mathias Shaw had his eyes and ears gathering intelligence around Azeroth. Now at least some eyes have come home.

Pets.Name: Crackleroar
Pets.Description: This small cub has been influenced by constant storms on Siren Isle. Just petting it's soft fur sends static shocks across your hand.

Pets.Name: Living Rose
Pets.Description: When being adored, a Living Rose emits beautifully intoxicating aromas. When ignored, those aromas become actually toxic.

Pets.Name: Spotty
Pets.Description: People get turned into rats sometimes. It's not that weird.

Pets.Name: Grunch
Pets.Description: It's best not to ask how Grunch acquired all these gifts because you know it's not because he's been nice.

Pets.Name: Mechagopher
Pets.Description: Do you see pity in these blinking, soulless eyes? I didn't think so.

Pets.Name: Cruncher
Pets.Description: The itchy legs! The wiggly antennae! Okay, fine, he's not cute. But he's trying!

Pets.Name: Lab Rat
Pets.Description: They weren't lying, this rat can absolutely get that dang cheese.

Pets.Name: Venture Companyman
Pets.Description: Yes boss! Yes boss! Yes boss!

Pets.Name: Blackwater Kegmover
Pets.Description: Being a pirate is alright with me, especially if you've got this thing willing to carry your grog.

Pets.Name: Steamwheedle Flunkie
Pets.Description: It's lame to have a hunchbacked crony mewl 'yes master' when you can just get a robot to do it for free.

Pets.Name: Mr. DELVER
Pets.Description: Originally built by Dr. DELVER to compete with renowned treasure hunter Brann Bronzebeard. Never really panned out though, so now he just helps out.

Pets.Name: Personal-Use Sapper
Pets.Description: Fun for the whole family! A self-detonating robot for all occasions!

Pets.Name: Bilgewater Junkhauler
Pets.Description: Scrap metal hunks hauling junk.

Pets.Name: Professor Punch
Pets.Description: And he was all like, Primal Punch!!

Pets.Name: Rocketfist
Pets.Description: And even if they reach the danish, they'll never get past Rocketfist!

Pets.Name: Viridian Mechasaur
Pets.Description: It totally fell into a pit of acid but then it came back ten times stronger! So cool!!!

Pets.Name: Crimson Mechasaur
Pets.Description: It's red because it like, killed so many bad guys! It's all their blood! And it's on fire too!

Pets.Name: Wavebreaker Mechasaur
Pets.Description: And then, woosh! It comes stampeding out of the ocean and like, roars! Raaargh!

Pets.Name: Mutt
Pets.Description: One more thing... Show my hound some respect.

Pets.Name: Goggles
Pets.Description: Do the hyenas need to wear goggles? Absolutely not. Does it look extremely cool? Incredibly.

Pets.Name: Eepy
Pets.Description: He's just a bit tuckered out. He's just a little guy.

Pets.Name: Mind Slurp
Pets.Description: One shudders to imagine whether the name was based off of what it is... or what it does.

Pets.Name: Brown Leafbug
Pets.Description: When you tell a bad joke, he'll be there to let you know.

Pets.Name: Rak-Ush Battleshell
Pets.Description: Raised as living tanks for Anub'azal's army, Rak-Ush's battleshells are surprisingly affectionate. Who's a good beetle?

Pets.Name: Guacamole
Pets.Description: This snuffling loves limes.

Pets.Name: Sneef
Pets.Description: She's got a schnoz for sniffin'!

Pets.Name: Snuffling
Pets.Description: Even hard sediment poses little challenge to the digging claws of these critters.

Pets.Name: Cinderwold Sizzlestinger
Pets.Description: Now that is one spicy bee!

Pets.Name: Sandstone Ramolith
Pets.Description: They loves sand. It's coarse, and it gets everywhere.

Pets.Name: Sir Shady Mrrgglton Junior
Pets.Description: The shadowy creature has been following Sir Finley Mrrgglton around ever since he happened upon it during one of his expeditions. Having grown quite fond of the tenebrous friend, Sir Finley won't part with it easily.

Pets.Name: Chester
Pets.Description: Part lean, mean candle-throwing machine. Part adventurer wannabe. All kobold.

Pets.Name: Blightbud
Pets.Description: Please keep your blightbud on a leash at all times! They are notorious for the havoc they can wreak on unprotected gardens.

Pets.Name: Loamy
Pets.Description: The softest and fluffiest protosheep made of the softest and fluffiest soil.

Pets.Name: Starkstripe Hopper
Pets.Description: Brightly colored means do not touch. Red is nature's warning light!

Pets.Name: Fathom Incher
Pets.Description: In the depths of the Endless Sea, strange thalassic horrors lurk. Not this fella, though. He's completely normal.

Pets.Name: Venomwing
Pets.Description: It's not the most original anti-hero name. But it promises to be a compelling crossover.

Pets.Name: Violet Sporbit
Pets.Description: Dangerous, toxic spores? What if it was purple?!

Pets.Name: Wriggle
Pets.Description: The wily fish has never been caught at the end of an angler's hook. He was caught a completely different way.

Pets.Name: Skippy
Pets.Description: Watch Skippy swim, and then watch him run. The perfect amphibian. Sorry, frogs.

Pets.Name: Fringe
Pets.Description: Someone once mistook Fringe for an azerite node. He's too young to remember azerite, though.

Pets.Name: Jump Jump
Pets.Description: I jump, Jon. It's what I do.

Pets.Name: Bouncer
Pets.Description: Awww, aren't it's fuzzy little pedipalps cute?

Pets.Name: Writhing Transmutagen
Pets.Description: The early worms get the bird.

Pets.Name: Itchbite
Pets.Description: A bug? Wait a minute, no, it's a small friendly spider!

Pets.Name: Spinner
Pets.Description: She'll try spinning, that's a good trick.

Pets.Name: Shadowbog Hopper
Pets.Description: Restoring hope to those lost in the darkness comes with its just rewards. Like cute frog friends.

Pets.Name: Oop'lajax
Pets.Description: He's a little jumpy... especially around the face.

Pets.Name: Bop
Pets.Description: Woah there pardner. Were you really about to buzz by without saying howdy?

Pets.Name: Argos
Pets.Description: The Imperial Lynx is known for being stubborn and moody, but fiercely loyal to those they form bonds with.

Pets.Name: Vanilla
Pets.Description: This treasured lynx is a firm supporter of no changes.

Pets.Name: Bean
Pets.Description: The toes! The huge toe beans! The huge toe beans are not for eating!

Pets.Name: Tiberius
Pets.Description: The Imperial Lynx hails from the shore of the far-flung Arathi Empire, where they serve as fierce and loyal companions and mounts for citizens.

Pets.Name: Rak-Ush Threadling
Pets.Description: Awww, aren't her pedipalps adorable?

Pets.Name: Lovely Duckling
Pets.Description: Of all the shades and colors of ducklings that come forth in the spring, the most prized are the pink ones. There are many theories about why, but it's likely just because pink is awesome.

Pets.Name: Snorr
Pets.Description: Wyvern whelps are sleepy by nature. The dreams of wyvern whelps are doubly so.

Pets.Name: Drowsey
Pets.Description: Is it a snail? Is it a dream? Is it the dream of a sleeping snail?

Pets.Name: Juniper
Pets.Description: Juniper's unique coloration makes it hard to hide from predators in the Emerald Dream's lush green foliage. She therefore made the wise decision to travel with you instead.

Pets.Name: Spruce
Pets.Description: What Spruce lacks in speed, she makes up for with sheer stubbornness.

Pets.Name: Elmer
Pets.Description: Elmer once ate a whole cake all by himself. It wasn't even his cake.

Pets.Name: Snoots
Pets.Description: Snoots has a supernatural sense of smell. It's not any better than normal, it's just supernatural.

Pets.Name: Somnolet
Pets.Description: Dream on. Dream until your dream comes true!

Pets.Name: Dustite
Pets.Description: Dustites scavenge among the roots of great trees for any small leavings they can find.

Pets.Name: Seedle
Pets.Description: Seedles burrow beneath plants in the emerald dreams and nibble at their roots.

Pets.Name: Snoozles
Pets.Description: Snoozles was once given a choice between life in the timeless beautiful snowfields and life in a warm home with pet treats. We know which one he chose.

Pets.Name: Napps
Pets.Description: This creature could sleep through another Cataclysm.

Pets.Name: Memorie
Pets.Description: It is said that some animals dream in black and white. This animal only dreams in green.

Pets.Name: Dreamhoof
Pets.Description: Be sure to sleep with some grass under your pillow so you can give this critter a snack while you dream.

Pets.Name: Dreamborne Scarab
Pets.Description: Compared to a fully grown dreamborne scarab, this is the lesser of two weevils.

Pets.Name: Reverie
Pets.Description: The duck of your dreams... or perhaps someone else's dreams.

Pets.Name: N'Ruby
Pets.Description: A lifetime of servitude to Anub'arak, then the Lich King, and now... you!

Pets.Name: Obsidian Warwhelp
Pets.Description: A war-obsessed world turned her into a weapon... but they couldn't break her spirit.

Pets.Name: Killbot 9000
Pets.Description: DEFECTIVE; REFUSES TO KILL. MARK FOR IMMEDIATE DESTRUCTION OR SALE TO LESS INTELLIGENT ENTITY.

Pets.Name: Doomrubble
Pets.Description: He seems to be very angry, but it's possible he's just hungry.

Pets.Name: Jeepers
Pets.Description: This eye has witnessed untold horrors, and these fangs may be responsible for a few of their own.

Pets.Name: Gill'dan
Pets.Description: Gill'dan earned his great power by drinking the blood of a demon, which would also have turned his scales green had they not already been that color.

Pets.Name: Briarhorn Hatchling
Pets.Description: Loves: Broccoli, playing, and smashing things. Hates: Meat, sleeping, and mean titan keepers who won't let you smash things.

Pets.Name: Rithro
Pets.Description: While napping in shrubbery, sometimes his snaggletooth gets caught on a leaf.

Pets.Name: Teardrop Moth
Pets.Description: Contrary to their name, these moths are actually quite happy.

Pets.Name: Heartseeker Moth
Pets.Description: Whereas some moths are attracted to light, this particular moth seems attracted to warm, fuzzy feelings. Some say the phrase "butterflies in your stomach" actually refers to an infestation of Heartseeker Moths.

Pets.Name: Aquapo
Pets.Description: The fury of the sea in the packge of a puddle.

Pets.Name: Skaarn
Pets.Description: Skaarn likes to coil up inside of your backpack.

Pets.Name: Ridged Shalewing
Pets.Description: For a creature made of rocks it hovers quite effortlessly.

Pets.Name: Thimblerig
Pets.Description: Known to hide money in its shell.

Pets.Name: Diamondshell
Pets.Description: These snails are especially prized by many drogbar snail-racers for being fast and looking fantastic.

Pets.Name: Brul
Pets.Description: Sure it's just a rock, but how much can it lift?

Pets.Name: Miloh
Pets.Description: Miloh likes naps. Also snacks.

Pets.Name: Devourer Lobstrok
Pets.Description: Fortunately it mainly devours pet treats.

Pets.Name: Cheddar
Pets.Description: It got its name because its favorite food is fondue.

Pets.Name: Lord Stantley
Pets.Description: Lord Stantley will arrive fashionably early to any party that serves shrimp.

Pets.Name: Crimson Swoglet
Pets.Description: Crimson swoglets are considered the most scholarly of all swoglets because they're well red.

Pets.Name: Ambre
Pets.Description: Nothing sparked joy in Ambre's heart until it found the perfect match.

Pets.Name: Blaise
Pets.Description: Like a campfire, but it wants skritches.

Pets.Name: Gilded Mechafrog
Pets.Description: An old and dangerous discarded toy.

Pets.Name: Pinkie
Pets.Description: With her distinctive coloration and impeccable work ethic, Pinkie has propelled herself to the top of the tallstrider fashion world.

Pets.Name: Troubled Tome
Pets.Description: Do you dare to open this book of draconic secrets?

Pets.Name: Gale Spirit
Pets.Description: When it was the wind, this spirit carried scents to every corner of Azeroth. Sometimes you can still smell on it the barest hint of food, smoke, or the carnage of battle.

Pets.Name: Blaze Spirit
Pets.Description: This is the soul of a forest fire. Though its armor keeps its flames under control, you sometimes notice it gazing longingly at dead trees and dry underbrush.

Pets.Name: Dust Spirit
Pets.Description: When it was captured and bound to a cage made of enchanted metal, this spirit managed to twist and reshape the metal into the suit of armor it now wears.

Pets.Name: Tide Spirit
Pets.Description: I wish that I could bind the tides and bend them to my will. But in its depths the sea abides and waits for no one still.

Pets.Name: Petal
Pets.Description: When the Bushbeard clan decided they were going to have a wedding, they chose Petal to be the flower girl. She was spectacular.

Pets.Name: Scout
Pets.Description: Scout came to the Dragon Isles for adventure and excitement. After finding you, he got both.

Pets.Name: Sophic Amalgamation
Pets.Description: A small fraction of the titans' power, an even smaller fraction of their size.

Pets.Name: Stormie
Pets.Description: As an egg, this proto-drake was struck by a freak bolt of lightning. Years later, the lightning still lives inside.

Pets.Name: Chip
Pets.Description: Born of living stone. Reforged by the hands of Wrathion's agents. Sadly much too tiny to make a difference in the black dragons' war against the djaradin.

Pets.Name: Roseate Hopper
Pets.Description: Most brightly colored frogs are incredibly poisonous. The Roseate Hoppers are different because they are only credibly poisonous.

Pets.Name: Sapphire Crab
Pets.Description: These crabs build their own shells from small fragments of rocks and gems.

Pets.Name: Bugbiter Tortoise
Pets.Description: This turtle eats bugs, as you'd expect from its name, but you wouldn't believe how large the bugs are.

Pets.Name: Snowclaw Cub
Pets.Description: An adult Snowclaw can rattle the very trees with its roar. A cub can rattle one of the smaller branches.

Pets.Name: Echo of the Cave
Pets.Description: Formed by the reckless use of elemental magic, the slumbering earth awakens. It is angry.

Pets.Name: Primal Stormling
Pets.Description: Raindrops whip around this creature with frightening speed, ripping and tearing anything that touches it. Experts say the best way to avoid injury is to give it a treat and some snuggles.

Pets.Name: Jeweled Emerald Whelpling
Pets.Description: A magically infused emerald sits at the heart of this creature.

Pets.Name: Jeweled Ruby Whelpling
Pets.Description: Trade Prince Gallywix wants you to know that he already owns three of these.

Pets.Name: Jeweled Amber Whelpling
Pets.Description: The hardest part to shape was the whelpling's cute little snout.

Pets.Name: Jeweled Sapphire Whelpling
Pets.Description: Sometimes you can see blue light flickering deep inside the gems that make up this strange creature.

Pets.Name: Kobaldt
Pets.Description: Sharp crystal needles protrude from this creature's back, and yet it still wants you to pet it somehow.

Pets.Name: Berylmane
Pets.Description: The vorquin of the dragon isles have manes that come in a variety of stunning colors.

Pets.Name: Patos
Pets.Description: This frillfish is a no-frills kind of frillfish.

Pets.Name: Emmah
Pets.Description: Is it normal for a fish to have fangs?

Pets.Name: Buckie
Pets.Description: Gnawing at the roots of magic trees have given this creature the ability to transcend time itself.

Pets.Name: Brightfeather
Pets.Description: It has bright feathers. What else is there to say?

Pets.Name: Blackfeather Nester
Pets.Description: There is no fury quite like that of a mother Blackfeather defending her nest.

Pets.Name: Yipper
Pets.Description: Soon after you put down this slyvern's decay-twisted mother, you learned the hard way both how it got its name and why Zon'Wogi asked you to care for it.

Pets.Name: Echo of the Heights
Pets.Description: In the mountain air, frost lingers upon the grass as a storm rolls in.

Pets.Name: Viridescent Duck
Pets.Description: These beautiful, elusive birds have very erratic migratory patterns, which can be difficult to track, leading some explorers to erroneously believe ducks simply didn't exist.

Pets.Name: Quack-E
Pets.Description: Beep. Beep. Beep. Quack.

Pets.Name: Mallard Duckling
Pets.Description: What do you feed a cute little duckling? What? No, not cheese and quackers! Quackers aren't healthy for ducks.

Pets.Name: Pilot
Pets.Description: Still learning how to fly.

Pets.Name: Echo of the Depths
Pets.Description: What the deep claims, none can reclaim.

Pets.Name: Ashenwing
Pets.Description: Red for the fires in which it was born.

Pets.Name: Phoenix Wishwing
Pets.Description: Some think this creature is smoldering because it is on fire. In truth it's because of its burning gaze.

Pets.Name: Scruffles
Pets.Description: Scruffles' lust for battle runs deep.

Pets.Name: Bunbo
Pets.Description: It sits anywhere it wants to, but it prefers to sit on laps.

Pets.Name: Echo of the Inferno
Pets.Description: A spark can become an ember. An ember can become a raging fire. This creature was never a spark or an ember, it has only ever been a raging fire.

Pets.Name: Ghostflame
Pets.Description: A dream of the memory of fire.

Pets.Name: Luvvy
Pets.Description: In its big eyes you can see your own reflection. You also see everything else because its eyes are so big.

Pets.Name: Shyfly
Pets.Description: These elusive creatures are famous for being hard to find unless you already know where to look. Fortunately, Haniko told you where to look.

Pets.Name: Pistachio
Pets.Description: Pistachio got her name in exactly the way you would expect.

Pets.Name: Chestnut
Pets.Description: Chestnut once found an acorn larger than he was, ate the whole thing, and doubled in size. It's a good thing he didn't eat a Trunkalumpf.

Pets.Name: Alvin the Anvil
Pets.Description: When Alvin said he wanted to be the best anvil ever, everyone doubted him. If anvils could laugh, he'd be the one laughing now.

Pets.Name: Jean's Lucky Fish
Pets.Description: To some, this fish speaks eloquently of its survival in the most arduous conditions. To most, it says 'glub.'

Pets.Name: Azure Frillfish
Pets.Description: Oddly enough, you met this fish drifting through the air.

Pets.Name: Wakyn
Pets.Description: Hope, like this phoenix, rises from the ashes of shattered dreams.

Pets.Name: Shaggy
Pets.Description: This little rabbit is cute from the tip of her button nose to the tips of her deadly claws.

Pets.Name: Jeweled Onyx Whelpling
Pets.Description: The master jewelcrafters of the Dragon Isles have perfected their craft so much that their gemmed statues can be brought to life. This whelp forged of obsidian is now not to be trifled with.

Pets.Name: Helpful Workshop Bot
Pets.Description: The name 'Beepbop' followed by the words 'Made by Threadis' are roughly but lovingly scratched into the bottom plate on this tiny nerubian-crafted bot.

Pets.Name: Microlicid
Pets.Description: Since synthesized creatures come into being fully grown, why would anyone design an immature helicid?

Pets.Name: Viperid Menace
Pets.Description: Where some viperids lie in wait for their prey, this variety stalks their intended meals for hours.

Pets.Name: Archetype of Vigilance
Pets.Description: Two parts fortress, one part eyestalks, one part slime.

Pets.Name: Leaping Leporid
Pets.Description: Left to their own devices, these leporids will leap high into the air at the slightest provocation. Sometimes they'll even come down properly, as well.

Pets.Name: Prototickles
Pets.Description: This creature's existence strongly implies the existence of other, more advanced tickle-based aurelids out there.

Pets.Name: Terror Jelly
Pets.Description: It feeds on fear, but is generally too cute to inspire terror on its own.

Pets.Name: Archetype of Renewal
Pets.Description: Just one part jelly, but refreshed over and over and over again.

Pets.Name: Stabilized Geomental
Pets.Description: Lihuvim's geomental creations are the most majestic of walking rock piles.

Pets.Name: Multichicken
Pets.Description: It's really unclear what's 'multi' about them.

Pets.Name: Violent Poultrid
Pets.Description: These poultrids are hostile, but never angry. They love every moment of every battle.

Pets.Name: Fierce Scarabid
Pets.Description: The red coloring on this scarabid lets potential opponents know it is not to be trifled with.

Pets.Name: Ambystan Darter
Pets.Description: Ambystans are swift but cowardly creatures. At the first hint of danger, a darter will hide in the nearest hole.

Pets.Name: Shelly
Pets.Description: Isn't she just the sweetest little helicid?

Pets.Name: Archetype of Satisfaction
Pets.Description: Two parts wings, one part beak, two parts talon, one part smugness.

Pets.Name: Archetype of Malice
Pets.Description: Two parts wings, one part dagger, five parts sadism.

Pets.Name: Archetype of Cunning
Pets.Description: Three parts planning, one part danger, one part noodle.

Pets.Name: Archetype of Survival
Pets.Description: One part shell. One part claw. As many parts resilience as need be.

Pets.Name: Archetype of Predation
Pets.Description: Eight parts legs. Eight parts eyes. One part rope. One part insatiable hunger.

Pets.Name: Archetype of Metamorphosis
Pets.Description: One part gills, one part haste, one part whatever it needs to be.

Pets.Name: Archetype of Multiplicity
Pets.Description: One part duplication, two parts duplication, four parts duplication... oops.

Pets.Name: Archetype of Serenity
Pets.Description: One part slippery skin, one part powerful legs, one part vocal sac, one part complete self-acceptance.

Pets.Name: Archetype of Animation
Pets.Description: One part rocks, two parts bigger rocks, three parts smaller rocks, one part ability to move anyway.

Pets.Name: Archetype of Motion
Pets.Description: One part beak, two parts wings, one part inability to stop, one part unwillingness to stop.

Pets.Name: Tunneling Vombata
Pets.Description: Only newly synthesized tunneling vombata have been seen. The rest are probably happily digging underground, never to be seen again.

Pets.Name: Archetype of Discovery
Pets.Description: One part facial sphere, one part cube manufactory, one part successful curiosity.

Pets.Name: Omnipotential Core
Pets.Description: It could become anything. It could become *everything*. In the meantime, it's... this.

Pets.Name: Resonant Echo
Pets.Description: The core of a song that all but faded long ago.

Pets.Name: Archetype of Focus
Pets.Description: One part focus, one part core, zero parts coherent or stable shape.

Pets.Name: Gnashtooth
Pets.Description: It only has two teeth, and they both mean business.

Pets.Name: Domestic Aunian
Pets.Description: A domesticated species, it is quite calm and seems hungry.

Pets.Name: Mosscoated Gromit
Pets.Description: The hide color of this species is more rare on Korthia, and helps it hide from predators. And mawsworn, in this case.

Pets.Name: Korthian Specimen
Pets.Description: With Korthia torn asunder by the Jailer, the few species that remain must be archived.

Pets.Name: Rook
Pets.Description: Rook never speaks, and little about them is known.

Pets.Name: Mawsworn Minion
Pets.Description: The Sanctum of Domination accepts mawsworn of all shapes, sizes, and levels of corporeality.

Pets.Name: Gilded Darknight
Pets.Description: The golden armor on these mawsworn is flimsy and easily bent -- the gilded armor is a punishment.

Pets.Name: Eye of Extermination
Pets.Description: The rage Zovaal harbored for his punished existence echoes through the eye.

Pets.Name: Eye of Allseeing
Pets.Description: The Eye of the Jailer, diffused, is now merely an all-seeing magical bauble.

Pets.Name: Chompy
Pets.Description: One of several indistinguishable pets, all named Chompy.

Pets.Name: Golden Eye
Pets.Description: See reflections on the water? There's more than darkness in the depths.

Pets.Name: Irongrasp
Pets.Description: There was once a mawsworn so vile that he was killed by his own minions. So great was his malice that a fragment of him lives on in this glove.

Pets.Name: Grappling Gauntlet
Pets.Description: Struggle all you want, it will just grip harder.

Pets.Name: Amaranthine Stinger
Pets.Description: Though the spawn of a gorm queen lost within the Maw, this pet appears to be untainted by the Jailer's foul magics.

Pets.Name: Invasive Buzzer
Pets.Description: Gorm find a way to invade just about anywhere, and the Maw is no exception.

Pets.Name: Fodder
Pets.Description: When you're a Maldraxxian stitchcrafter and you have to name a lot of abominations, sometimes you just go with the obvious choice.

Pets.Name: Rarity
Pets.Description: One particular Broker has taken a liking to the skrat species, kept this rare specimin as a pet, and inherited the smell as a souvenir.

Pets.Name: Gizmo
Pets.Description: Gizmo is considered a perfect purrkin, perhaps the best that has ever existed. He is also a ruthless murderer of small critters.

Pets.Name: Topaz Baubleworm
Pets.Description: Baubleworms have no natural predators, and grow their gemstones to attract mates.

Pets.Name: Turquoise Baubleworm
Pets.Description: Baubleworms were found by the Brokers on an unknown world, and so gorgeous were their gems, that quickly the expedition turned against one another.

Pets.Name: Ruby Baubleworm
Pets.Description: The gemstones on a baubleworm will quickly deteriorate if the worm is killed. Healthy worms are kept as prized pets by wealthy Brokers.

Pets.Name: Copperback Etherwyrm
Pets.Description: The brilliant scales of the etherwyrm reflect the bright skies of Bastion.

Pets.Name: Sly
Pets.Description: Sly snuck aboard for a trip into the Maw. He's not scared, he just really wants to stay in your backpack for a while.

Pets.Name: Timeless Mechanical Dragonling
Pets.Description: Dragons come in all types of temporal classification -- timeless, time-lost, and even infinite.

Pets.Name: Infused Etherwyrm
Pets.Description: Infused etherwyrms can be used as portable anima batteries, but they must be trained not to flee at the sight of combat.

Pets.Name: Lil' Abom
Pets.Description: The fields of battle teem with Lil'Abom parts, which are perfectly interchangable and easy to put back together.

Pets.Name: Flawless Amethyst Baubleworm
Pets.Description: Baubleworms are highly prized in the Veiled Market, where those featuring few gemstone flaws can fetch extreme prices.

Pets.Name: Squibbles
Pets.Description: Squibbles is engineered to never leave your side.

Pets.Name: Lucy
Pets.Description: Lucy was sent to Revendreth, as all cats are, and somehow escaped. When questioned, local dredgers blamed it on her being a cat.

Pets.Name: Courage
Pets.Description: Young larions are often given to aspirants as an early test of compassion and leadership.

Pets.Name: Larion Cub
Pets.Description: When fully-grown, a larion that has grown up with an aspirant will never leave his or her master's side.

Pets.Name: Larion Pouncer
Pets.Description: Larion are prideful beasts, and see anyone that enters their territory as a challenge.

Pets.Name: Lost Featherling
Pets.Description: Tales say that the stewards were created to aid the denizens of Bastion in their eternal duties. They don't seem to mind.

Pets.Name: Steward Featherling
Pets.Description: Stewards looking to move up in steward society know to fluff up their feathers, give out tons of juice, and always be hooting.

Pets.Name: Archivist's Quill
Pets.Description: A good friend to Archivist Fane since his earliest days, Janeera is ready for him to let the quill go as she feels he is getting too attached.

Pets.Name: Spinebug
Pets.Description: You got spinebugged!

Pets.Name: Putrid Geist
Pets.Description: Despite being decrepit and undead, geists move with exceptional speed and agility.

Pets.Name: Iridescent Ooze
Pets.Description: This slime seems to change color the more you look at it, and then suddenly, it appears to be looking back at you. Perhaps it is time for a rest.

Pets.Name: Spinemaw Gormling
Pets.Description: While young, they have an adorable face and cute little antennae. While grown up, they gain razor sharp pinchers and are unstoppable eating machines. Life is full of compromises.

Pets.Name: Sharpclaw
Pets.Description: The razor sharp claws will tear into anything with ease, making for an excellent emergency can opener.

Pets.Name: Decaying Mawrat
Pets.Description: Tiny flakes of skin seem to perpetually fall off of this horrible creature, leaving behind a trail and a very distinct, foul odor.

Pets.Name: Undying Deathroach
Pets.Description: This species has a very distinct set of markings on its back that makes it easy to identify in the unending horrors of the Maw.

Pets.Name: Dread
Pets.Description: Staring at one of these creates a deep void of emotion at the very core of your being, then fills it with a sense of dread.

Pets.Name: Lavender Nibbler
Pets.Description: Poking a tiny gormlng in the side makes a very satisfying, squishy noise. It is NOT advisable to poke one too many times.

Pets.Name: Torghast Lurker
Pets.Description: That eerie feeling you get when the shadows seem to shift and follow you isn't just a feeling, it's usually one of these.

Pets.Name: Maw Stalker
Pets.Description: These guardians of Torghast are always watching for suspicious activity.

Pets.Name: Frenzied Mawrat
Pets.Description: Its teeth are razor sharp and will chew through just about anything.

Pets.Name: Ashen Chomper
Pets.Description: Sometimes it is best to leave what strange creature you found in a mysterious jar in the depths of Torghast alone.

Pets.Name: Maw Crawler
Pets.Description: The mysterious forces that animate this mobile mitt don't seem particularly evil, yet.

Pets.Name: Severs
Pets.Description: If a detached hand claps by itself inside of Torghast, can the Jailer hear it?

Pets.Name: Hissing Deathroach
Pets.Description: Many have asked where these creatures came from, and how they made it to the Maw, but none that researched them have ever been heard from again.

Pets.Name: Tower Deathroach
Pets.Description: Torghast is a tower of hardship and perpetual agony, which is a perfect environment for these creatures.

Pets.Name: Carpal
Pets.Description: Nobody knows why all of these skeletal hands are from the left hand. Where did the right hands go?

Pets.Name: Char
Pets.Description: The skies may have scorched part of Revendreth, but persistent creatures are still able to exist in these harsh conditions.

Pets.Name: Sir Reginald
Pets.Description: No one is quite sure where Reginald got his or her name, but what we do know is they don't answer to anything else.

Pets.Name: Shimmerbough Hoarder
Pets.Description: These critters are capable of holding a tremendous amount of food in their cheeks for easy transportation between hiding spaces.

Pets.Name: Runelight Leaper
Pets.Description: This pet has tremendous airborne agility and will always land on its feet, even when flung by a home-made spring-loaded leaper-flinger.

Pets.Name: Stemmins
Pets.Description: Whatever you do, don't ask it to carry a bucket of water. We don't talk about what happened to that area of the Shadowlands.

Pets.Name: Leafadore
Pets.Description: These saplings grow unnaturally quickly when given enough anima, and their leaves are razor sharp when fully grown.

Pets.Name: Fun Guss
Pets.Description: This particular tiny tree is infested with strange spores and doesn't seem to mind.

Pets.Name: Bloodlouse Larva
Pets.Description: What vile subtance is jiggling around inside of this creature?

Pets.Name: Stony
Pets.Description: Abandoned by its master in a secluded room, the little bat has been hibernating in stone for ages.

Pets.Name: Battie
Pets.Description: Roosting over Sinfall, Battie was roused one evening by the music of the first Ember Court. She has not missed a single one since.

Pets.Name: Sinfall Screecher
Pets.Description: Joining with their new residents in Sinfall a few of these juvenile dredbats accompanied Renathal's forces into the maw.

Pets.Name: The Count
Pets.Description: Roosting in the grounds of Castle Nathria, The Count did not foresee falling into an eternal stone slumber.

Pets.Name: Lost Quill
Pets.Description: No longer lost, it's now considering a name change.

Pets.Name: Stoneskin Dredwing Pup
Pets.Description: These dredbats are built by the Stonewright for the Court of Harvesters. They are bonded to their owners and become fierce fighters.

Pets.Name: Bloodfeaster Spiderling
Pets.Description: Unlike most spiders that drain all the fluids from their prey, the bloodfeaster has developed a taste for blood alone.

Pets.Name: Foulwing Buzzer
Pets.Description: The barbed mandibles on this species will dig deep into anything they bite, causing a lot of irritation.

Pets.Name: Mu'dud
Pets.Description: The soul that occupies Mu'dud was a harsh ruler in life, and now he seeks to dominate the most violent kingdom of all.

Pets.Name: Toenail
Pets.Description: If you can beat them, stitch them!

Pets.Name: Sludge Feeler
Pets.Description: It is not advisable to hug a sludge feeler. Those stains never wash out.

Pets.Name: Invertebrate Oil
Pets.Description: Is the ooze holding the bones in, or are the bones holding the ooze together?

Pets.Name: Micromancer
Pets.Description: Even the smallest bones don't go to waste in Maldraxxus.

Pets.Name: Backbone
Pets.Description: What it lacks in appendages, it makes up for in positive attitude.

Pets.Name: Writhing Spine
Pets.Description: Some predators in Maldraxxus collect spines as trophies, and some of them don't stop moving even then.

Pets.Name: Feasting Larva
Pets.Description: Being thrown into a pit of bloodlouse larvae is a painful fate for those that fail the great houses of Maldraxxus.

Pets.Name: Corpselouse Larva
Pets.Description: Though they are quite squishy while young, the bloodlouse will eventually grow hardened plates around its entire body.

Pets.Name: Plaguelouse Larva
Pets.Description: A parasite that latches onto some of the larger beasts of Maldraxxus and drinks until fully-grown, one wonders how the beasts don't manage to notice.

Pets.Name: Shy Melvin
Pets.Description: One of Plague Deviser Marileth's first creations, Shy Melvin was too timid for the demands placed on slimes by the House of Plagues.

Pets.Name: Bubbling Pustule
Pets.Description: The bubbles inside of these ooze creatures make a very satisfying pop noise when they finally surface and rupture.

Pets.Name: Skittering Venomspitter
Pets.Description: If this species spits venom at its prey, is it still technically considered venom, or just really gross?

Pets.Name: Boneweave Hatchling
Pets.Description: Maldraxxus is full of mighty and terrifying creatures, causing most denizens to be abnormally calm when around the indigenous species of giant spiders.

Pets.Name: Luminous Webspinner
Pets.Description: The bright color of the luminous webspinner taunts would-be predators to chase them into their webs.

Pets.Name: Pernicious Bonetusk
Pets.Description: The tusks on these creatures are fused to their skulls in such a manner that they feel no pain when charging or ramming into anything.

Pets.Name: Corpulent Bonetusk
Pets.Description: A newborn bonetusk has no visible tusks, but within weeks their tusks grow to a size proportional to their body.

Pets.Name: Oonar's Arm
Pets.Description: The thrill of battle and the grip of war have not yet left the bones of Oonar.

Pets.Name: Spirited Skyfoal
Pets.Description: Few things are as adorable as a young skyfoal.

Pets.Name: Indigo
Pets.Description: The mane on this tiny foal reflects a majestic indigo color when the light hits it in just the right way.

Pets.Name: Devoured Wader
Pets.Description: When newly-hatched, they start with colorless, white feathers. Within weeks, they flourish into a wide spectrum of beautiful colors.

Pets.Name: Gilded Wader
Pets.Description: These golden-feathered creatures do not lay golden eggs, for that you would need a golden goose.

Pets.Name: Purity
Pets.Description: Despite having a very fine coat of fur, young vulpin do not shed at all.

Pets.Name: Goldenpaw Kit
Pets.Description: The golden vulpin are notorious for loud, barking yowls that echo across the plains of Bastion.

Pets.Name: Sable
Pets.Description: Vulpin actually have very light-colored skin, but their incredibly thick coats hide any color from showing through.

Pets.Name: Silvershell Snapper
Pets.Description: Terrapins prefer any sort of berry as their primary source of food, though they will eat almost anything.

Pets.Name: Brightscale Hatchling
Pets.Description: These brightly-colored terrapins are the least commonly seen in the wild. It is considered a sign of luck if you rub the top of its shell.

Pets.Name: Helpful Glimmerfly
Pets.Description: A glimmerfly can load up nearly fifty percent of its weight as pollen and still retain full agility while flying.

Pets.Name: Golden Cloudfeather
Pets.Description: A group of teroclaws is known as a fleece.

Pets.Name: Pearlwing Heron
Pets.Description: The whooping noise these pets make when hungry will rival any tormented cry you can find in Torghast.

Pets.Name: Devouring Animite
Pets.Description: No one knows where the devourers came from, but it is theorized that they flourish on several lost land masses in the In-Between.

Pets.Name: Gorm Harrier
Pets.Description: If you count the number of times its wings beat per minute, you can calculate how long it will be until this little terror will sting you.

Pets.Name: Gorm Needler
Pets.Description: There are more sharp pointy things on this species of gorm than there are on a Horde capital city.

Pets.Name: Grubby
Pets.Description: These little slugs will try to eat literally anything they scoot over.

Pets.Name: Gloober, as G'huun
Pets.Description: Even the terrifying G'huun was, perhaps, once this cute.

Pets.Name: Hungry Burrower
Pets.Description: While these gormlings may not be the fastest diggers, they are very efficient and can move long distances underground before tiring.

Pets.Name: Willowbreeze
Pets.Description: When the winds blow just right, howling through the canyons of Ardenweald, you can find these foals running with the wind in a spectacular show of prowess and color.

Pets.Name: Starry Dreamfoal
Pets.Description: When a full moon is visible, streaks of hair on the dreamfoal's mane start to shimmer and glow like shooting stars.

Pets.Name: Fuzzy Shimmermoth
Pets.Description: Shimmermoths don't actually shimmer in the sunlight. Nobody knows who named these, but they have a lot to answer for.

Pets.Name: Dusty Sporeflutterer
Pets.Description: The moths of Ardenweald can carry seeds and pods many times their own weight, aiding the Night Fae in their eternal tasks.

Pets.Name: Amber Glitterwing
Pets.Description: Much like how a squid can squirt ink to escape in the water, a glitterwing can shake the dust off of its wings and similarly vanish.

Pets.Name: Floofa
Pets.Description: Floofa smells like citrus fruits when its fur is wet.

Pets.Name: Cloudfeather Fledgling
Pets.Description: These ferocious flying creatures scrape their beaks against hard surfaces to sharpen them and intimidate foes.

Pets.Name: Chirpy Valeshrieker
Pets.Description: Despite the adorable name, the chirp is more of a horrible, spine-tingling shriek.

Pets.Name: Rocky
Pets.Description: The claws of a vulpin sharpen themselves naturally when walking around on any sort of cobblestone or rocky surface.

Pets.Name: Dodger
Pets.Description: Dodger is a bit small relative to other vulpin, but is excellent at playing hide and seek.

Pets.Name: Violet Dredwing Pup
Pets.Description: A rare mutation gives this dredbat its violet underbelly, making it a sought after gift for pet collectors with eclectic tastes.

Pets.Name: Crimson Dredwing Pup
Pets.Description: A wild dredbat, the crimson pups are tough to tame. Their elusiveness and hardiness set them apart from their kin.

Pets.Name: Trapped Stonefiend
Pets.Description: Trapped in a chest and forgotten for years, this stone fiend is mad and looking for a fight.

Pets.Name: Dal
Pets.Description: A fellow message courier to Chip, Dal was always on time until one day when he did not return from his appointed round.

Pets.Name: Court Messenger
Pets.Description: The stone fiends were created to serve as messengers and spies for the realm of Revendreth, and those of the Court of Harvesters serve that role extremely well.

Pets.Name: Raw Emotion
Pets.Description: These creatures manifest in the catacombs beneath Revendreth due to vile rituals practiced on souls there.

Pets.Name: Wrathling
Pets.Description: Drawn from an overabundance of pride and wrath these manifestations seek even more to sustain themselves.

Pets.Name: Primordial Bogling
Pets.Description: The sludge that can give rise to the dredgers has been known to coalesce and run a muck.

Pets.Name: Burdened Soul
Pets.Description: A soul burdened with the sins of its past.

Pets.Name: Animated Tome
Pets.Description: Beware awakening the power of knowledge.

Pets.Name: Bound Lightspawn
Pets.Description: Lightspawn can always be found on the battlefields where light touches, no matter what form it takes.

Pets.Name: Blushing Spiderling
Pets.Description: The crypts and tombs of Revendreth offer the perfect spider breeding ground, and Tomb Burster's brood has become fiercely territorial of their foothold.

Pets.Name: PHA7-YNX
Pets.Description: Inspired by the phalynx of Bastion, Engineers have modified the design to be approximately 300% more adorable.

Pets.Name: Windfeather Chick
Pets.Description: Despite what the young pandaren may tell you, the windfeather cranes do not actually have feathers made out of wind.

Pets.Name: Jade Defender
Pets.Description: Several adventurous miners have gone after the jade quilen in search of riches, but none have ever been heard from again.

Pets.Name: Wicked Lurker
Pets.Description: Three legs, two arms, three eyeballs, the extras are for emergency situations only.

Pets.Name: Cursed Dune Watcher
Pets.Description: This little guy was exiled and cursed by other watchers of Uldum to spend the rest of its time as a tiny insignificant being.

Pets.Name: Pygmy Camel
Pets.Description: These Pygmy Camels are often mistaken for Mysterious Camel Figurines.

Pets.Name: Aqir Tunneler
Pets.Description: These Aqir Scarabs are masters at tunneling through any terrain.

Pets.Name: Rotbreath
Pets.Description: Dragonbreath Chili is nothing compared to the stench coming from the mouth of this thing.

Pets.Name: Corrupted Tentacle
Pets.Description: Where did it come from, where does it go? Where did you come from, creepy tentacle?

Pets.Name: K'uddly
Pets.Description: His tentacles give you the best hug your brain has ever felt.

Pets.Name: Void-Scarred Beetle
Pets.Description: The shell on these beetles is quite brittle from the void interference, but it also has a strangely fast regeneration rate.

Pets.Name: Anomalus
Pets.Description: This ooze leaves behind a slimy trail as it moves that has a stench that would rival a beast rotting in the dunes of Uldum for a thousand years.

Pets.Name: Void-Scarred Rat
Pets.Description: A recent debate has risen up about whether Void-Scarred rats are better or worse than plagued rats.

Pets.Name: Void-Scarred Cat
Pets.Description: This cat eats anything from random scraps of food to strange glowing cubes of immense power. Be careful in front of its mouth.

Pets.Name: Void-Scarred Hare
Pets.Description: Only a being of immense power could scar such an adorable creature, unless this hare was already approaching the darkness...

Pets.Name: C'Thuffer
Pets.Description: It is always C'Thuffer. Always.

Pets.Name: Gloop
Pets.Description: It is very unclear whether this slime started with all of those eyes inside of it, or if those are "leftovers".

Pets.Name: Eye of Corruption
Pets.Description: This creepy eye is looking at everyone, always, and only has to blink once every 10,000 years.

Pets.Name: Ra'kim
Pets.Description: The winged aqir are very fast and agile, and their wings provide a nice breeze on a warm afternoon.

Pets.Name: Aqir Hivespawn
Pets.Description: The aqir hive has countless numbers of beings in it, all of which are supportive of the ever-present Hivemind.

Pets.Name: Void-Scarred Anubisath
Pets.Description: N'Zoth's corruption spread across many beings, including this little Anubisath.

Pets.Name: Muar
Pets.Description: The thirst for mana is insatiable, luckily this little one doesn't consume a whole lot.

Pets.Name: Plagueborn Slime
Pets.Description: In Maldraxxus, plague has a strange habit of finding its way back to you.

Pets.Name: Void-Scarred Pup
Pets.Description: This pup is now more than just a bundle of fur and teeth, it is fur, teeth and void.

Pets.Name: Void-Scarred Toad
Pets.Description: Pulled from a horrific vision of Orgrimmar these toads are soaked in void energy.

Pets.Name: Crimson Skipper
Pets.Description: This butterfly is stained red from the blood of its enemies.

Pets.Name: Sunsoaked Flitter
Pets.Description: The wings of this butterfly start off with no distinct color, but slowly change to yellow as they spend time in the sunlight.

Pets.Name: Papi
Pets.Description: Legend has it that these purple butterflies came from a place called Devil's Island.

Pets.Name: Armored Vaultbot
Pets.Description: A walking vault that throws its money at you is not a very secure vault to own.

Pets.Name: Slimy Sea Slug
Pets.Description: Slimy sea slugs secrete slimy sea slug slime on the slimy sea floor.

Pets.Name: Slimy Hermit Crab
Pets.Description: The slime on this provides essential nutrients to the hermit crab within, allowing it to go years without eating anything if it chooses.

Pets.Name: Slimy Darkhunter
Pets.Description: Living so deep within the seas has made this species very sensitive to lights.

Pets.Name: Slimy Fangtooth
Pets.Description: A fully grown Fangtooth has a bite strong enough to go right through a platinum fishing pole. Watch your fingers.

Pets.Name: Slimy Octopode
Pets.Description: This little octopode squirts slime out instead of ink.

Pets.Name: Slimy Eel
Pets.Description: Eels are already slimy. This one is extra slimy.

Pets.Name: Slimy Otter
Pets.Description: The slimy coating on this otter provides excellent protection against sunburns.

Pets.Name: Irradiated Elementaling
Pets.Description: In case of darkness, this little elemental is an infinite source of a soothing green glow.

Pets.Name: Adventurous Hopling
Pets.Description: The last thing that tried to look in its tiny pack was never seen again.

Pets.Name: Ghostly Whelpling
Pets.Description: Unkillable dragons, what could go wrong?

Pets.Name: Spraybot 0D
Pets.Description: Even he cannot put out the fire in my heart.

Pets.Name: Echoing Oozeling
Pets.Description: It doesn't take a very large ooze to eat a gnome.

Pets.Name: Arachnoid Skitterbot
Pets.Description: No one knows who made these mechanical spiders, but everyone hates them.

Pets.Name: Bonebiter
Pets.Description: Absolutely do not let him bite your bones.

Pets.Name: Microbot 8D
Pets.Description: He's just so happy to be programmed to exist.

Pets.Name: Microbot XD
Pets.Description: One of the microbots used to guard the city of Rustbolt, now purchasable for a reasonable price.

Pets.Name: Utility Mechanoclaw
Pets.Description: As handy as this invention is, it was also programmed to absolutely despise hand puns.

Pets.Name: Lost Robogrip
Pets.Description: It's not clear who the original owner of this arm was, but they're not getting it back now.

Pets.Name: OOX-35/MG
Pets.Description: Many advancements have been made to the initial prototype homing beacon over the years.

Pets.Name: Mustyfur Snooter
Pets.Description: A rare snooter, and hard to find, it's known for burrowing itself deep under wet foliage to nest.

Pets.Name: Snowsoft Nibbler
Pets.Description: Of all snooters, this one has the softest fur. Ironically, it does not like to be pet very much.

Pets.Name: Golden Snorf
Pets.Description: The birth of an exceptionally rare Golden Snorf was such an abnormality that not even King Mechagon could force himself to mechanize his beloved, fuzzy friend.

Pets.Name: Drowned Hatchling
Pets.Description: When the waves crashed down around the city, not all were fortunate enough to receive N'zoth's gift.

Pets.Name: Skittering Eel
Pets.Description: The eels of Nazjatar are experts at hiding in the many cracks and rocks of the sea floor.

Pets.Name: Coral Lashling
Pets.Description: The predatory coral of Nazjatar can grow incredibly quickly given the right conditions.

Pets.Name: Budding Algan
Pets.Description: There's no mistaking the evidence -- algan breed by budding. We can all put this argument to rest now.

Pets.Name: Brinestone Algan
Pets.Description: Standing next to a brinestone algan will remind you of the smell of the sea, mixed with rotting kelp.

Pets.Name: Scalebrood Hydra
Pets.Description: The hydra matriarchs of Nazjatar steal and destroy one another's eggs constantly, so you can never really know what will hatch out of one.

Pets.Name: Sandkeep
Pets.Description: This pile of animated sand is fun to squish between your fingers, but doesn't really like it when you do that.

Pets.Name: Stormwrath
Pets.Description: The Tidestone would empower minions of both the storm and the sea, and is sought after by both parties.

Pets.Name: Seafury
Pets.Description: The elements clash and battle for relics constantly, but few moreso than the Tidestone.

Pets.Name: Wriggler
Pets.Description: Watch your ears.

Pets.Name: Damplight Slug
Pets.Description: The bright coloration of this deep water slug seems to give off a soft, warm glow.

Pets.Name: Prismatic Softshell
Pets.Description: Even with its softer juvenile shell, a young prismatic spireshell still glitters like a diamond.

Pets.Name: Amethyst Softshell
Pets.Description: Spireshells produce a softer shell when young which will be grown out of quickly.

Pets.Name: Nameless Octopode
Pets.Description: The influence of the old gods on the aquatic life of Nazjatar is unmistakable, as many simply wander as mindless eyes of N'zoth.

Pets.Name: Daggertooth Frenzy
Pets.Description: Frenzies in Nazjatar have learned to operate in large schools or face consumption by the many predators of the deeps.

Pets.Name: Mindlost Bloodfrenzy
Pets.Description: In the fathomless depths beyond the Eternal Palace, minds are lost in the blackness.

Pets.Name: Spawn of Nalaada
Pets.Description: In a dark place unknown to all, the seabed writhes with the children of Nalaada, a carpet of life and death.

Pets.Name: Lightless Ambusher
Pets.Description: In the lightless caverns, a bright shell color doesn't make you any worse at ambushing.

Pets.Name: Chitterspine Devourer
Pets.Description: These gruesome crabs are attracted to the scent of blood and seem to revel in combat.

Pets.Name: Caverndark Nightmare
Pets.Description: As if a dark, underwater cave wasn't nightmarish enough, these things exist -- and they love it there.

Pets.Name: Chitterspine Needler
Pets.Description: A rare evolution of chitterspine, with elongated spines to pierce soft flesh and eyes.

Pets.Name: Pearlescent Glimmershell
Pets.Description: When the sun shines on its shell just right, the light scatters in a thousand different directions.

Pets.Name: Sandclaw Nestseeker
Pets.Description: Anything can be a nest, if you try hard enough.

Pets.Name: Glittering Diamondshell
Pets.Description: A rare species of crab found mostly in Nazjatar, it hides easily amongst the ample kelp and coral of the area.

Pets.Name: Necrofin Tadpole
Pets.Description: Legend has it that the Necrofin tribe found necromancy on a series of runed tablets at the bottom of the sea.

Pets.Name: Murgle
Pets.Description: The murloc tribe that Murgle belongs to is one of few that have resisted the naga's enslavement.

Pets.Name: Zanj'ir Poker
Pets.Description: Master of poking, stabbing, thrusting, jabbing, and gouging.

Pets.Name: Ravenous Prideling
Pets.Description: As a spawn of the most dangerous sha, this little monster has some big plans, but needs to wait a few thousand years first.

Pets.Name: Living Amber
Pets.Description: The mantid are extremely reliant upon amber for many things, and even use it in some of their magic.

Pets.Name: Spawn of Garalon
Pets.Description: The kunchong are extremely aggressive and will eat just about anything they can as soon as they are able to walk. If they can't eat it, they will smash it instead.

Pets.Name: Amberglow Stinger
Pets.Description: The glow eminating from these is both calming and eerie at the same time when in total darkness.

Pets.Name: Kor'thik Swarmling
Pets.Description: Don't let these eat after midnight, legend has it that it's very dangerous.

Pets.Name: Spirit of the Spring
Pets.Description: The elemental spirits of Pandaria have been known to plan tricks to play on unsuspecting visitors years in advance.

Pets.Name: Azure Windseeker
Pets.Description: This little cloud serpent's favorite activity is chasing its own tail.

Pets.Name: Baoh-Xi
Pets.Description: For being an inanimate object for many centuries, this statue sure moves around really well.

Pets.Name: Comet
Pets.Description: The Engine of Nalak'sha is defended by many mighty beings, and many tiny beings.

Pets.Name: Wayward Spirit
Pets.Description: Crossing into the spirit world too often can be dangerous for your health.

Pets.Name: Stoneclaw
Pets.Description: Quilen were the favored companions of the ancient Mogu race due to their loyal nature.

Pets.Name: Scaley
Pets.Description: Saurolisk scales are a surprisingly flexible but durable material that are often used to reinforce or mend things that are broken or worn through.

Pets.Name: Froglet
Pets.Description: The skin of a teal frog sometimes shimmers just right in the sunlight, providing excellent camouflage when on the surface of the water.

Pets.Name: Beakbert
Pets.Description: Beakbert's favorite food is a ravenberry.

Pets.Name: Mr. Crabs
Pets.Description: Mr. Crabs was victorious in a lengthy brawl amongst nearly a dozen other hermit crabs to claim his current shell.

Pets.Name: Violet Abyssal Eel
Pets.Description: The Abyssal Eels are able to swallow and digest something up to twice the width of their body.

Pets.Name: Cobalt Raven
Pets.Description: The feathers of the cobalt raven glow eerily in the moonlight, making them easy to spot during the night.

Pets.Name: Crimson Bat Pup
Pets.Description: Unlike most other bat species, the crimson bats are actually blind and rely entirely on echolocation to get around.

Pets.Name: Albatross Hatchling
Pets.Description: The Albatross is capable of flying further than any other species of bird on all of Azeroth.

Pets.Name: Rebuilt Mechanical Spider
Pets.Description: Anyone serious about using a mechanical spider in battle knows that you need to buy replacement legs in bulk. Those things are fragile.

Pets.Name: Rebuilt Gorilla Bot
Pets.Description: The convenient part of robots that fight each other is that you can just put them back together. Step two is to attach a giant laser cannon to ensure you win the next battle.

Pets.Name: Nightwreathed Watcher
Pets.Description: It is an honor in the Sentinel Army to be entrusted with the care of an owl born from the boughs of the great tree Teldrassil.

Pets.Name: Crimson Octopode
Pets.Description: The crimson species of octopode grows larger and hardier than others, and is capable of sustaining crushing pressure in the depths.

Pets.Name: Thunderscale Whelpling
Pets.Description: These whelps are frequently captured by the denizens of Jorundall when still young and trainable.

Pets.Name: Scritches
Pets.Description: Key activities include: rolling over, sunning self, whimpering for belly scratches, and enjoying belly scratches.

Pets.Name: Tonguelasher
Pets.Description: It has one solution to all of its problems: give em' a lashing.

Pets.Name: Lord Woofington
Pets.Description: Lord Woofington was given his title after the rest of the denizens of Havenswood succumbed to madness.

Pets.Name: Firesting Buzzer
Pets.Description: Firesting wasps are said to have one of the worst bites of any wasp species, causing a pain like a burning blaze.

Pets.Name: Shadefeather Hatchling
Pets.Description: Though barely a hatchling, its claws are already sharp enough to pierce the flesh of many smaller critters.

Pets.Name: Albino Duskwatcher
Pets.Description: Albino ravens are incredibly rare and highly prized amongst the traders of the Great Sea.

Pets.Name: Needleback Pup
Pets.Description: The needleback porcupines have survived life in island climates by evolving to be extra lucky.

Pets.Name: Rotting Ghoul
Pets.Description: Get it while it lasts.

Pets.Name: Leatherwing Screecher
Pets.Description: We don't know much about this species, but we do know this: bats aren't bugs.

Pets.Name: Baby Stonehide
Pets.Description: The playful kicks of these baby moose are enough to knock an orc flat on his back.

Pets.Name: Zur'aj the Depleted
Pets.Description: Once a mighty warrior of the n'raqi, Zur'aj's power was stolen and absorbed by an unknown rival.

Pets.Name: Void Jelly
Pets.Description: This Jelly has seen better days. Now it sees through fifty different eyes.

Pets.Name: Everburning Treant
Pets.Description: A lone Ancient of Lore in Darnassus managed to save this Treant in its final actions with a magical aura to prevent the flames from burning it any further.

Pets.Name: Hydrath Droplet
Pets.Description: Hydrath Water Droplets are also an excellent skin moisturizer.

Pets.Name: Detective Ray
Pets.Description: This is what happens when you try to one-up Pip Quickwit's tall tales of being stuck inside of dangerous creatures.

Pets.Name: Gust of Cyclarus
Pets.Description: When Cyclarus came to Darkshore, small cyclones appeared all over the place. Some of them dissipated over time, others found their way back to Cyclarus and were absorbed into the surrounding winds.

Pets.Name: Darkshore Sentinel
Pets.Description: The Sentinel Owls of Darkshore are forever protective of their homeland.

Pets.Name: Proper Parrot
Pets.Description: This upper class parrot is only for the most distinguished of sailors.

Pets.Name: Tanzil
Pets.Description: During certain times of the day, the sunlight can cause a reflection on the feathers of the red tinted raptors causing them to look more like flames than feathers.

Pets.Name: Trecker
Pets.Description: One of the earliest recorded victories in a large battle on Zandalar depicted mighty warriors riding into battle on armored raptors. The legend has it that the warrior did not choose their raptor, but that the raptor would choose the warrior.

Pets.Name: Dasher
Pets.Description: Like many other species, you can get a rough guess of how large the raptor will be as a fully grown adult by the size of its largest claw.

Pets.Name: Alarm-O-Dog
Pets.Description: The Alarm-O-Dog was built for security purposes, but people really wanted one for their home because they are so darn cute.

Pets.Name: Leper Rat
Pets.Description: The radiation from all of the machinery in Gnomeregan gives these rats a soft green glow when looked at in absolute darkness.

Pets.Name: Mechanical Cockroach
Pets.Description: Mechanical Cockroaches are less likely to survive an apocalypse than an actual living cockroach, making this one of the more questionable feats of engineering.

Pets.Name: Mechantula
Pets.Description: The Mechantula was created by an unknown gnomish Engineer. Rumor has it when the original schematic was found, it was in a room covered with dusty old webbing, but neither the original Mechantula or the engineer who drafted the schematic could be found.

Pets.Name: Thunder Lizard Runt
Pets.Description: The large plates on the back of this lizard are made of a mysterious but highly conductive material, making it ideal to charge up and expel massive amounts of electricity.

Pets.Name: Spawn of Krag'wa
Pets.Description: A toad with Krag'wa's bloodline has an average expectancy to leap over seven million times during its lifetime.

Pets.Name: Child of Pa'ku
Pets.Description: There is a story told to some children that all winged creatures were unable to fly until Pa'ku provided enough wind to lift them off of the ground.

Pets.Name: Feathers
Pets.Description: Thought to be lost in a massive explosion, the smartest of engineers were able to rebuild Feathers into a magnificent mechanical creation.

Pets.Name: Redridge Tarantula
Pets.Description: The tarantulas that inhabit southern Redridge are smaller than their cousins elsewhere in Azeroth, but make up for their size with speed and ferocity.

Pets.Name: Baby Crawg
Pets.Description: The horns of a crawg grow at a tremendous rate starting almost immediately after they are born, and is among the hardest of natural materials found on a living species.

Pets.Name: Bloated Bloodfeaster
Pets.Description: The smallest of cuts can be devastating to this species, they start to leak all over the place.

Pets.Name: Slippy
Pets.Description: These creatures are able to control each arm individually, which allows for great multi-tasking, and the ability to give the best hugs.

Pets.Name: Gearspring Hopper
Pets.Description: Mechanical Toads can hop over seven million times before their gears begin to wear out.

Pets.Name: Sunscale Hatchling
Pets.Description: The bright scales of this rare wind serpent soak up the sunlight to keep it warm, even in shade.

Pets.Name: Snapper
Pets.Description: Wear gloves with this little guy, he loves to bite!

Pets.Name: Littlehoof
Pets.Description: Littlehoof likes: bounding, bouncing, frolicking, and headbutts, in that order.

Pets.Name: Thistlebrush Bud
Pets.Description: The sprites that inhabit the islands of the Great Sea have thought of many traps to lure in unwary visitors.

Pets.Name: Laughing Stonekin
Pets.Description: The earth that's rumbling beneath your feet... is it quaking or laughing, or both?

Pets.Name: Mischievous Zephyr
Pets.Description: "Beware the cool breeze, for it brings trickery." - pandaren proverb

Pets.Name: Giggling Flame
Pets.Description: The most nefarious of the trickster spirits, and the reason why the pandaren invented fireproof pants.

Pets.Name: Playful Frostkin
Pets.Description: You know how disappointing it is when you return to your beverage and the ice has all melted? This guy's fault.

Pets.Name: Deathsting Scorpid
Pets.Description: It's sting is worse than... well nothing, really. Are you sure you want to keep this as a pet?

Pets.Name: Sandshell Chitterer
Pets.Description: During the warm summer months, the noise that these pests make is enough to drive a person mad.

Pets.Name: Octopode Fry
Pets.Description: A baby octopode is technically referred to as a "squish."

Pets.Name: Inky
Pets.Description: Whatever the story of his mysterious eyepatch is, he's not talking.

Pets.Name: Voru'kar Leecher
Pets.Description: The voru'kar are fiercely loyal to their leader, Vorus'arak, who led them to safety from the Lich King's grasp.

Pets.Name: Snort
Pets.Description: Particularly partial to coastal scrub grasses, this hardy species has survived many years in uncharted lands.

Pets.Name: Muskflank Calfling
Pets.Description: Getting just one of these fragrant beasts damp is the olfactory equivalent to rolling around in a pile of wet dogs.

Pets.Name: Coldlight Surfrunner
Pets.Description: The coldlight once were quite common across the oceans, to the grumbles of many hunters, but no longer.

Pets.Name: Barnaby
Pets.Description: A wealthy pirate captain met with a strange man on the docks of an unknown city and bartered for everlasting life for his faithful companion. As with most monkey-related wishes, something went wrong.

Pets.Name: Captain Nibs
Pets.Description: Captain Nibs was once the leader of an ill-fated sea voyage of slightly-too-smart-for-their-own-good monkeys that quickly capsized.

Pets.Name: Kunchong Hatchling
Pets.Description: Based on the size, the mantid appear to be re-growing their swarm, likely due to the presence of a new empress.

Pets.Name: Juvenile Brineshell
Pets.Description: Even young brineshell are drawn to the line in the sky that meets the waves, it calls them.

Pets.Name: Poro
Pets.Description: Poro cannot fly, but enjoys a long lifespan hopping along the ground and shaking the fruits from trees.

Pets.Name: Bloodstone Tunneler
Pets.Description: Is that a venomous worm in your pocket or... why is that in your pocket?

Pets.Name: Tinder Pup
Pets.Description: The only balls this hound chases are diamond-hard, fireproof rocks. Very heavy, not fun to throw.

Pets.Name: False Knucklebump
Pets.Description: Someone put this mechanical gorilla on the island to monitor the species, but who, and why?

Pets.Name: Craghoof Kid
Pets.Description: The hooves of this subgroup of goats have evolved to be equally surefooted on sand or stone.

Pets.Name: Kindleweb Spiderling
Pets.Description: The kindleweb spider species is known amongst the island tribes for its warm, threadable webbing.

Pets.Name: Sparkleshell Sandcrawler
Pets.Description: The shells of this particular group of crabs shimmer like gems on the sand.

Pets.Name: Scuttle
Pets.Description: Scuttle was named after the ships he destroys, not his movement patterns, and don't you forget it.

Pets.Name: Lil' War Machine
Pets.Description: A mighty war machine of adorable proportions.

Pets.Name: Lil' Siege Tower
Pets.Description: A mighty war machine of adorable proportions.

Pets.Name: Squawkling
Pets.Description: Boulderfist legend recounts a cloud of carrion birds following ogre warriors through the Dark Portal during the Second War; it may be that all two-headed scavengers in Azeroth are their descendants.

Pets.Name: Fozling
Pets.Description: A living piece of Arathi's most infamous stone-faced behemoth, Fozruk.

Pets.Name: Azerite Puddle
Pets.Description: These drops of Azerite are volatile and prone to explosive reactions when prodded.

Pets.Name: Foulfeather
Pets.Description: Plaguefeather and her brood feed well on the carrion the battle for Stromgarde has left scattered across the Highlands.

Pets.Name: Ragepeep
Pets.Description: Part owl, part bear... and part stag, which handily avoids potential lawsuits.

Pets.Name: Scabby
Pets.Description: While Yogursa managed to survive the blight infesting many of Hillsbrad's ursines, she was driven mad, and her cubs forever tainted.

Pets.Name: Teeny Titan Orb
Pets.Description: Constructs designed to assist the titans with sundry small tasks, this example is oddly small. Teeny Titan Orb! Let's Go!

Pets.Name: Voidwiggler
Pets.Description: Either Darbel Montrose was a terrible warlock, or she simply adored cute things. Perhaps both.

Pets.Name: Aldrusian Sproutling
Pets.Description: So long as one of his Sproutlings remains, Branchlord Aldrus will never truly die.

Pets.Name: Fuzzy Creepling
Pets.Description: The young of Arathi Highlands' plains spiders may kick clouds of barbed abdomen hairs into predators' eyes. All this, and poisonous web-spitters, too.

Pets.Name: Azeriti
Pets.Description: A chip off the ol' girl's block. Azeriti is fiercely protective and will strike out against anyone wishing to do her mother harm.

Pets.Name: Carnivorous Lasher
Pets.Description: This horrific, flesh-eating plant promises it can make all of your wildest wishes come true. If you feed it.

Pets.Name: Cou'pa
Pets.Description: He is a real trooper, faithfully tagging along with Kojo for years. He enjoys hide and seek as well as some serious shell sliding.

Pets.Name: Sandstinger Wasp
Pets.Description: It is a well-established fact that wasps produce far higher quality honey than bees. Well-established in very, very small circles of wasp farmers.

Pets.Name: Barnacled Hermit Crab
Pets.Description: She just wanted to get away. Find a nice shell. Sit on a beach. Now she's your pet.

Pets.Name: Crimson Frog
Pets.Description: This frog's peculiar red color appears to have evolved specifically for camouflage in blood. Spooky.

Pets.Name: Freshwater Pincher
Pets.Description: Finding freshwater pearls with this crab by your side will be a pinch.

Pets.Name: Blue Flitter
Pets.Description: So pretty you could eat it. But don't. It's poisonous.

Pets.Name: Saurolisk Hatchling
Pets.Description: They are so cute before they develop razor-sharp teeth and venom.

Pets.Name: Accursed Hexxer
Pets.Description: No one knows where this haunted tiki mask came from, or why its so angry all the time. It seems to like you though!

Pets.Name: Swamp Toad
Pets.Description: These toads do not appreciate having their legs pulled off.

Pets.Name: Child of Jani
Pets.Description: The scavenger Loa thinks you can teach this saurid a thing or two. Just don't lose sight of your coin purse.

Pets.Name: Ranishu Runt
Pets.Description: Always hungry, and has a shockingly good personality.

Pets.Name: Lil' Ben'fon
Pets.Description: White Brutosaurs were on the brink of extinction until some enterprising adventurers took action. Lil' Ben'fon may have inherited her father's size, but definitely has her mother's temper!

Pets.Name: Tragg the Curious
Pets.Description: Krag'wa reluctantly sent Tragg to the Talanji Expedition with the hope that they could find someone suitable to escort him on a tour of Azeroth.

Pets.Name: Bloodfeaster Larva
Pets.Description: Exceptionally squishy, especially after a big meal.

Pets.Name: Zandalari Shinchomper
Pets.Description: Known to chomp on the shins of everything from saurids to brutosaurs.

Pets.Name: Tiny Direhorn
Pets.Description: A popular pet among children, despite frequent accidental gorings.

Pets.Name: Wicker Pup
Pets.Description: She loves tummy rubs. Just watch out for splinters.

Pets.Name: Mechanical Prairie Dog
Pets.Description: This adorable mass of whirlers, cogs, and sprockets wasn't assembled in Gnomeregan. Where then?

Pets.Name: Greatwing Macaw
Pets.Description: Best not to park your mount beneath this one.

Pets.Name: Bilefang Skitterer
Pets.Description: Silver dust sparkling on each of this spider's legs attracts prey; unwittingly drawn into an inescapable web.

Pets.Name: Frenzied Cottontail
Pets.Description: This red-nosed bunny is incredibly soft to the touch; just watch your fingers.

Pets.Name: Drustvar Piglet
Pets.Description: This piglet avoided being turned into sausage at the hands of Roland with the help of a cunning spider.

Pets.Name: Corlain Falcon
Pets.Description: Corlain Falcons are trained from birth to be scouts and fighting companions. The few spared from the Heartsbane curse serve the Order of Embers in the same capacity.

Pets.Name: Seabreeze Bumblebee
Pets.Description: These hardy bees form the backbone of a great honey and mead industry. For decades, the Tidesages forbade anyone from taking them outside of Stormsong.

Pets.Name: Abyssal Eel
Pets.Description: When the Naga invaded Stormsong, they brought their favored pets: Abyssal Eels.

Pets.Name: Enchanted Tiki Mask
Pets.Description: The Zandalari are masters of magic and communications with the spirits, and they often imbued items with powers to strengthen their bonds with the spirit world.

Pets.Name: Restored Revenant
Pets.Description: The Drustvar people learned over time that when burying someone or some thing that there are sadly a number of shady people looking to exhume and reanimate whatever they can find.

Pets.Name: Taptaf
Pets.Description: Taptaf is loyal beyond a doubt, as long as food is provided.

Pets.Name: Direhorn Hatchling
Pets.Description: The Direhorn species is among the toughest and strongest of all things that walk on Azeroth. They can seem like gentle giants, but be cautious that you don't find yourself in front of one when they are on a warpath.

Pets.Name: Lil' Tika
Pets.Description: Most predators favored the soft parts of their prey to attack first.  Lil' Tika prefers to go for the face.

Pets.Name: Vengeful Chicken
Pets.Description: The wisest engineers of Azeroth all know that the only thing more dangerous than a living evil chicken, is the mind of an evil chicken transplanted into the body of a mechanical chicken.

Pets.Name: Guardian Cobra Hatchling
Pets.Description: A newly hatched Cobra can be just as deadly as a fully grown one, if not more.

Pets.Name: Smoochums
Pets.Description: Smoochums loved life in Glenbrook, it was full of people to play with and places to sleep, and also where unspeakable acts happen at the most innocent of tea parties.

Pets.Name: Poda
Pets.Description: Poda was just a small part of the larger business, always trying to live up to the standards of his older brother Coda.

Pets.Name: Filthy Slime
Pets.Description: You're not entirely sure where this came from, nor do you really want to know, but you are sure that it does eat a tremendous amount of garbage.

Pets.Name: Spawn of Merektha
Pets.Description: Not much is known about the great snake Merektha and the many eggs in the Temple of Sethraliss, but the residents of Zandalar all seem to have a distinct amount of both respect and fear for these serpents.

Pets.Name: Silithid Mini-Tank
Pets.Description: This silithid battle tank evolved prematurely. It will never reach full size, but it is just as ferocious as its larger cousins.

Pets.Name: Dart
Pets.Description: This young crawgling was rescued from a vicious blood troll war camp. With care, and lots of feeding, you earned his trust and Krag'wa asked you to take care of him always.

Pets.Name: Rebellious Imp
Pets.Description: What are they rebelling against? The real question is what are they NOT rebelling against.

Pets.Name: Fossorial Bile Larva
Pets.Description: If a bile larva stays burrowed for an extended period of time they begin to lose their pigment. When they finally do emerge, they can be quite ravenous.

Pets.Name: Grasping Manifestation
Pets.Description: Reaching out from some vast unknown corner of the void... to give you a hug.

Pets.Name: Fel-Afflicted Skyfin
Pets.Description: Watch out when it spits.

Pets.Name: Docile Skyfin
Pets.Description: Skyfin can be observed in a wide variety of colors. Biologists believe the vibrancy of those colors both help attract mates and ward predators.

Pets.Name: Fel Lasher
Pets.Description: Beautiful and deadly.

Pets.Name: Unstable Tendril
Pets.Description: Its friends call it "Slappy."

Pets.Name: Corrupted Blood
Pets.Description: Some fragments of Deathwing's blood live on to this day, deep within the lost places of the world, forever seeking their lost host.

Pets.Name: Faceless Mindlasher
Pets.Description: Can't quite take over your mind, but capable of giving you a really bad migraine.

Pets.Name: Infernal Pyreclaw
Pets.Description: During his time in the Firelands, Fandral would often grow bored and work shaping magic on the local flora and fauna.

Pets.Name: Surger
Pets.Description: Five things he can never do without: surging, surging, surging, surging, surging.

Pets.Name: Cinderweb Recluse
Pets.Description: A rarely-seen cinderweb species known for devouring her mate's legs and laying eggs in his still-twitching body, and a lovely purple color.

Pets.Name: Blazehound
Pets.Description: Some say it's the pup of Riplimb and Rageface, others say 'I don't want to think about that.'

Pets.Name: Drafty
Pets.Description: If you feel a slight gust that made you shiver, that was probably Drafty.

Pets.Name: Zephyrian Prince
Pets.Description: A minor diety of wind, lording over the tiny breezes that make you think the heat of the summer season will be bearable for a brief moment.

Pets.Name: Faceless Minion
Pets.Description: It has no eyes, but it always seems to be staring at you.

Pets.Name: Bound Stream
Pets.Description: Amongst the rudest of the Twilight's Hammer's corrupting activities was binding the elements of small streams and brooks.

Pets.Name: Twilight Clutch-Sister
Pets.Description: Even very small things can be corrupted, the apocalypse is for everyone.

Pets.Name: Rattlejaw
Pets.Description: Nefarian's reanimated bone constructs were an effective way to combat the many adventurers that plagued him, and especially their mages.

Pets.Name: Discarded Experiment
Pets.Description: Maloriak was nothing if not resourceful, not letting even small scraps of flesh go to waste.

Pets.Name: Tinytron
