Pets.Description: During their years-long war with the black dragonflight, the dwarves tried crafting smaller golems that were more difficult to hit.

Pets.Name: Ghost Shark
Pets.Description: Queen Azshara's curse over the denizens of Azsuna did not only affect the night elves. This shark is doomed to an eternity of bottomless hunger.

Pets.Name: Ageless Bronze Drake
Pets.Description: This particular drake has oddly retained its age throughout its travels, but its size has certainly changed.

Pets.Name: Bronze Proto-Whelp
Pets.Description: The Bronze Proto-Whelps have been discovered only in the most peculiar of timelines.

Pets.Name: Dig Rat
Pets.Description: Rogues often kept a few of these in their pockets in times of emergency. Don't ask me why.

Pets.Name: Sneaky Marmot
Pets.Description: This marmot has been with you this entire time. He's that good.

Pets.Name: Orphaned Felbat
Pets.Description: Sure are a lot of these around the Broken Shore.

Pets.Name: Scraps
Pets.Description: He's one cowardly companion, because he's got no guts...

Pets.Name: Naxxy
Pets.Description: It's just constantly barfing plague fluid. Where does it come from? Where does it go?

Pets.Name: Dibbler
Pets.Description: This tiny robot specializes in drilling, clamping, and shooting lightning out of its brain. A typical gnome invention.

Pets.Name: Young Venomfang
Pets.Description: Untouched by the deviate corruption seeping through the Wailing Caverns.

Pets.Name: Cavern Moccasin
Pets.Description: This serpent seems used to being at your side, it may have been the companion of one of the other druids of the fang.

Pets.Name: Everliving Spore
Pets.Description: In the fertile grounds of the Wailing Caverns, nothing truly dies.

Pets.Name: Albino Buzzard
Pets.Description: Crysa has collected many exotic avians in her travels, none more rare than this hatchling.

Pets.Name: Wicked Soul
Pets.Description: An unrepentant soul that was once trapped inside Frostmourne, and now flits restlessly between worlds.

Pets.Name: Drudge Ghoul
Pets.Description: One of innumerable ghouls summoned by the Lich King in his final battle, left to roam the world for eternity.

Pets.Name: Soulbroken Whelpling
Pets.Description: The Lich King tore the spirits of Sindragosa's lost whelps into undeath in front of her skeletal form as a final affront to the great dragon.

Pets.Name: Blightbreath
Pets.Description: The eternal answer to the question 'what's that smell?'

Pets.Name: Blood Boil
Pets.Description: It really seems to want to touch you, but why?

Pets.Name: Boneshard
Pets.Description: A small fragment of Lord Marrowgar, it will NOT stop assaulting your shins.

Pets.Name: Creeping Tentacle
Pets.Description: There is something deeply unsettling about the tentacle, perhaps it is best not to look at it for too long.

Pets.Name: G0-R41-0N Ultratonk
Pets.Description: The next level of scrapbot technology: pushes the scrap into a heap, then destroys it with flame vents and rockets.

Pets.Name: Snaplasher
Pets.Description: Glowing from within, these sprouts have been blessed with a gift from Eonar.

Pets.Name: Winter Rageling
Pets.Description: In the eternal balance of frost and fire, the stolid ice forms a defense without measure.

Pets.Name: Sanctum Cub
Pets.Description: Rub its belly more than twice and you're just asking to be pounced on.

Pets.Name: Runeforged Servitor
Pets.Description: The runeforged sentries of Ulduar march endlessly without the need for rest or recharging.

Pets.Name: Ironbound Proto-Whelp
Pets.Description: The denizens of Ulduar transformed even small whelps into ironclad monstrosities.

Pets.Name: Magma Rageling
Pets.Description: In the eternal balance of frost and fire, the raging flames fuel many aggressive fronts.

Pets.Name: Nerubian Swarmer
Pets.Description: Thousands of these skittering nightmares swarm underneath Northrend to this day.

Pets.Name: Snobold Runt
Pets.Description: Really much more tolerable when he's not riding on your head.

Pets.Name: Dreadmaw
Pets.Description: Deep beneath the Argent Tournament grounds a clutch of eggs belonging to mates Acidmaw and Dreadscale was found.

Pets.Name: Igneous Flameling
Pets.Description: Who's ready for a hot stone massage?

Pets.Name: Noblegarden Bunny
Pets.Description: Why do bunnies lay eggs?

Pets.Name: Benax
Pets.Description: Younger brother to Anax.  Big brother to Cenax and Denax.

Pets.Name: Court Scribe
Pets.Description: Ghosts are voracious readers.

Pets.Name: Corgnelius
Pets.Description: True royalty among Corgis.

Pets.Name: Firebat Pup
Pets.Description: Fire it up!

Pets.Name: Ash'ana
Pets.Description: One of Ash'alah's litter, entrusted to you by the Dreamweavers.

Pets.Name: Hungering Claw
Pets.Description: Thousands of years ago, ten magisters were exiled from Highborne society for seeking power in the corrupting darkness. In the wilds, they found hunger, desperation, and Kosumoth.

Pets.Name: Lurking Owl Kitten
Pets.Description: It's a bird! It's a cat! It's... biting me...

Pets.Name: Untethered Wyrmling
Pets.Description: A wyrmlings temperment hinges primarily on how long its been since its fed on mana. Remember to feed your pets.

Pets.Name: Transmutant
Pets.Description: When alchemy goes wrong... So, so, wrong.

Pets.Name: Sting Ray Pup
Pets.Description: These fast and nimble swimmers are highly desired by pet collectors. Though they can appear friendly, their tail stinger can kill on contact.

Pets.Name: Pygmy Owl
Pets.Description: No size jokes, please.

Pets.Name: Felbat Pup
Pets.Description: A felbat's fire never burns its master.

Pets.Name: Wyrmy Tunkins
Pets.Description: In his home realm, he commanded an army of ten million demons. Now his power is yours to command. Go make him fight a chicken!

Pets.Name: Trigger
Pets.Description: Ever wanted to cuddle with a gun? Introducing Trigger, the Cuter Shooter.

Pets.Name: Sunborne Val'kyr
Pets.Description: Val'kyr are considered "sunborne" when they are honored in the presence of Odyn.

Pets.Name: Baby Elderhorn
Pets.Description: Isn't baby elderhorn an oxymoron?

Pets.Name: Leyline Broodling
Pets.Description: When demons, nightmares, and magic collide!

Pets.Name: Knockoff Blingtron
Pets.Description: Don't want to break the bank on that new robot? Have a little helper at half the cost! Come down to Goblin Experimental Engineering Korporation and put in your order! All sales final.

Pets.Name: Alarm-o-Bot
Pets.Description: For Sale: Slightly used Alarm-o-Bot. Rebuilt and refurbished. Still annoying.

Pets.Name: Risen Saber Kitten
Pets.Description: Eternally cute.

Pets.Name: Thistleleaf Adventurer
Pets.Description: Leaving the forest to see the world, which must look so much bigger to such a little guy.

Pets.Name: Fetid Waveling
Pets.Description: After a long night out mages find that their water elementals smell a bit off.

Pets.Name: Empowered Manafiend
Pets.Description: First created in the presence of Highmaul runes, this tiny manafiend really packs a punch!

Pets.Name: Empyreal Manafiend
Pets.Description: A shimmering creature made of pure arcane energy, it is hard to focus your eyes directly on it.

Pets.Name: Energized Manafiend
Pets.Description: Energized by the ogre-mages of Highmaul, this mana-hungry creature will drain you dry before you know it.

Pets.Name: Fel Piglet
Pets.Description: Even piglets are not safe from the Legion's corruption.  Although, this one seems harmless enough.

Pets.Name: Thaumaturgical Piglet
Pets.Description: The formula had a bit too much... purple.

Pets.Name: Ridgeback Piglet
Pets.Description: He loves to play in the mud! Then he likes to give you a hug!

Pets.Name: Plump Jelly
Pets.Description: Once its tentacles take hold there is no escape, much like its love for you.

Pets.Name: Sewer-Pipe Jelly
Pets.Description: Dalaran's drainage is not as good as you think.

Pets.Name: Bleakwater Jelly
Pets.Description: When a helsquid has devoured most of the food in any area, it can return to its polyp form and begin life anew.

Pets.Name: Crispin
Pets.Description: Oddly, he was named Crispin even before the incident.

Pets.Name: Nursery Spider
Pets.Description: Why is it called a nursery spider? Does it prefer to lurk in nurseries? Why would it do that? What does it do there? Oh, dear! Someone check on the kids!

Pets.Name: Grumpling
Pets.Description: Grumplings are created when the tears of a kidnapped child fall upon a mound of new snow.

Pets.Name: Nightmare Whelpling
Pets.Description: What's a little corruption between friends?

Pets.Name: Stormborne Whelpling
Pets.Description: Its scales are electrified, making it exceedingly tricky to cuddle.

Pets.Name: Emmigosa
Pets.Description: The great-great-great-great-great granddaughter of Senegos.

Pets.Name: Eye of Inquisition
Pets.Description: Hello. Is it me you're looking for?

Pets.Name: Extinguished Eye
Pets.Description: What was once a great enemy is now an adorable friend!

Pets.Name: Fledgling Warden Owl
Pets.Description: A young companion in need of training.

Pets.Name: Skyhorn Nestling
Pets.Description: A very rare bird, but this one was intrusted to you.

Pets.Name: Ashmaw Cub
Pets.Description: This little guy has had a rough start at life, be sure to take good care of him.

Pets.Name: Grumpy
Pets.Description: You saved him, but maybe he saved you.

Pets.Name: Enchanted Pen
Pets.Description: Strega the Hag, tired of writing thank you notes, used her magic to automate the task.  Unfortunately, the pen had a dark side and all the recipients received strongly worded letters...

Pets.Name: Enchanted Torch
Pets.Description: Strega the Hag didn't like carrying her own torches.  One quick enchantment, and the thing would dutifully follow her into the night.

Pets.Name: Enchanted Cauldron
Pets.Description: Strega the Hag enchanted her cauldron to prevent it from tipping over and spilling hot soup.  She never expected it to achieve sentience and attempt to murder her.

Pets.Name: Blazing Firehawk
Pets.Description: Arakkoa sun worship has resulted in the creation of some strange and monstrous minions.

Pets.Name: Savage Cub
Pets.Description: Pets often look a lot like their owners, which is especially true if the owner was a saberon.

Pets.Name: Shard of Cyrukh
Pets.Description: A small amount of Cyrukh burns everlasting within this portion of the Cipher. Best keep it close to you.

Pets.Name: Crusher
Pets.Description: Found wandering in a small blizzard, this lost goren carries a mining pick to show that he is a jack of all trades.

Pets.Name: Left Shark
Pets.Description: Who would leave such a cute shark behind? The Iron Horde truly has no soul.

Pets.Name: Corrupted Nest Guardian
Pets.Description: There is very little difference between a corrupted nest guardian and an omelette chef.

Pets.Name: Ghostshell Crab
Pets.Description: These crabs have developed an advanced form of camouflage that causes their skin to appear translucent.

Pets.Name: Nightmare Bell
Pets.Description: Once a holy artifact used by draenei priests to calm spirits, it has become animated by anguished souls and drawn to locations of death and corruption.

Pets.Name: Periwinkle Calf
Pets.Description: These playful little riverbeasts enjoy scooping up a large mouthful of water and then spitting it at one another.

Pets.Name: Cinder Pup
Pets.Description: Found munching on a blackened bone in the Black Forge of Blackrock Foundry, this pup really seems to warm up to you quickly.

Pets.Name: Lost Netherpup
Pets.Description: Rumor has it that some beasts that were touched by the void exist in a space between this world and the next.

Pets.Name: Young Talbuk
Pets.Description: Talbuk grow their horns to full size at an early age.

Pets.Name: Slithershock Elver
Pets.Description: The rivers of Draenor are filled with moray.  They are vicious predators, attacking anything that enters the water.

Pets.Name: Wretched Servant
Pets.Description: Magic-addicted elves too small and frail to serve in Kil'Jaeden's army were relegated to servant roles for the higher-class demons.

Pets.Name: Chaos Pup
Pets.Description: The Twisting Nether is an infinite abyss of terrors and one small corner of extreme cuteness.

Pets.Name: Sunblade Micro-Defender
Pets.Description: Because you don't want to know what happens when a rat wanders into the Sunwell.

Pets.Name: Hyjal Wisp
Pets.Description: Hundreds of these wisps came together to defend Hyjal from Archimonde and save the world.

Pets.Name: Stinkrot
Pets.Description: What's that smell?

Pets.Name: Sister of Temptation
Pets.Description: She's the devil on your shoulder, whispering in your ear.

Pets.Name: Fragment of Desire
Pets.Description: It's only somewhat interested.

Pets.Name: Fragment of Suffering
Pets.Description: It's only mildly irritated.

Pets.Name: Fragment of Anger
Pets.Description: It's just a little mad.

Pets.Name: Abyssius
Pets.Description: A fragment of the great abyssal Supremus, Abyssius contains enough fiery essence to burn a hole right through your pocket.

Pets.Name: Leviathan Hatchling
Pets.Description: The dark green water in the Black Temple's sewer system gives these hydras their distinctive color.

Pets.Name: Grotesque
Pets.Description: A smaller gargoyle that is used to divert the flow of rainwater on scourge buildings to prevent mausoleum flooding.

Pets.Name: Servant of Demidos
Pets.Description: Demidos created a great many servants when he was hosting parties for other void lords.

Pets.Name: Bone Serpent
Pets.Description: Young bone serpents will try to burrow into a living creature's skull and devour their brain.  So you may want to wear a night cap.

Pets.Name: Glowing Sporebat
Pets.Description: Wild sporebats are vicious creatures, but if tamed at a young age, they can be loyal companions.

Pets.Name: Kaliri Hatchling
Pets.Description: Hunted to near extinction for its feathers, the kaliri has developed keen senses.

Pets.Name: Veilwatcher Hatchling
Pets.Description: Hunted to near extinction for its feathers, this kaliri has developed keen senses.

Pets.Name: Dusty Sporewing
Pets.Description: Their wings emit a fine powder as a defensive mechanism when it is in danger.

Pets.Name: Bloodthorn Hatchling
Pets.Description: Ravagers are favored ogre snacks when they are small and less deadly.

Pets.Name: Eye of Observation
Pets.Description: You'll always feel like... something is watching you.

Pets.Name: Sky Fry
Pets.Description: Playing fetch with a ray is always fun.  You don't even have to aim.

Pets.Name: Son of Sethe
Pets.Description: Godlike being now in travel size.

Pets.Name: Albino River Calf
Pets.Description: Their white hide would make them vulnerable to sunburn, if it wasn't as thick as plate armor.

Pets.Name: Sunfire Kaliri
Pets.Description: Legend says the Sunfire Kaliri flies into the sun itself when it is ready to die.

Pets.Name: Soul of the Forge
Pets.Description: When forging enchanted steel, bits of magic seep into the coals.  Over a period of years, decades even, the unbound energy slowly takes form, creating a tiny elemental being of immense fortitude.

Pets.Name: Puddle Terror
Pets.Description: Living so closely to the water's surface, the puddle terror is not only friend to all aquatic creatures, it is one.

Pets.Name: Sentinel's Companion
Pets.Description: The companion of a night elf sentinel, rarely given out as a gift.

Pets.Name: Everbloom Peachick
Pets.Description: These tiny creatures seem to favor big, fluffy nests, preferably atop an animal-loving dryad.

Pets.Name: Mechanical Scorpid
Pets.Description: Iron, iron, steel and fire.  Bellows, gears, plates, and wire.

Pets.Name: Doom Bloom
Pets.Description: A clear indication why goblin fertilizers should be used sparingly.

Pets.Name: Bronze Whelpling
Pets.Description: The Bronze Whelpling was lost in time until an unusual event allowed her to escape.

Pets.Name: Firewing
Pets.Description: Don't line her cage with paper unless you want a fire.

Pets.Name: Hatespark the Tiny
Pets.Description: His hate is not diminished by size.

Pets.Name: Frostwolf Pup
Pets.Description: These pups are bundles of fur and teeth until they grow up, at which point they are really just teeth.

Pets.Name: Hydraling
Pets.Description: The hydraling can be an expensive pet, as it is capable of eating from three food bowls at the same time.

Pets.Name: Brilliant Spore
Pets.Description: Brilliant spores are very rarely seen, found deep under the waves of Draenor within the darkest corners of the Zangar fungal encroachment.

Pets.Name: Seaborne Spore
Pets.Description: A spore from Zangarra, a central area of the Zangar encroachment that would later bloom into the Zangarmarsh.

Pets.Name: Umbrafen Spore
Pets.Description: The deep blue Umbrafen would later become home to a tribe of Broken.

Pets.Name: Crimson Spore
Pets.Description: A spore plucked from the Crimson Fen, an area of Gorgrond that was swallowed when Draenor was torn apart by nether magics.

Pets.Name: Zangar Spore
Pets.Description: In Draenor, the Zangar Sea seeks to overtake the land by releasing thousands of these spores and slowly growing inland over time.

Pets.Name: Lanticore Spawnling
Pets.Description: No one knows what deep part of Blackwing Descent the Lanticore was kept in, but it probably smelled pretty awful.

Pets.Name: Ikky
Pets.Description: Kaliri hatchlings feast on grubs when they're young, and eventually graduate to anything smaller than themselves.

Pets.Name: Ancient Nest Guardian
Pets.Description: The ancient nest guardian was designed with the unthinkable in mind: to hate cats.

Pets.Name: Frostwolf Ghostpup
Pets.Description:
The little wolf told to stay.
Watching, waiting, for vacant prey.
Too proud to leave, she slipped away.
Unburdened now, forever free to play.

Pets.Name: Netherspawn, Spawn of Netherspawn
Pets.Description: This slime has a long and confusing lineage.

Pets.Name: Widget the Departed
Pets.Description: Not even death would stop this kitten's insatiable curiosity.

Pets.Name: Cursed Birman
Pets.Description: One of this cat's nine lives has returned to haunt the living by yowling loudly when you're trying to sleep.

Pets.Name: Stout Alemental
Pets.Description: The Pandaren Brewmasters accidentally brought a barrel of Uncle Gao's last sentient batch of brew with them to Brewfest.

Pets.Name: Blazing Cindercrawler
Pets.Description: Tiny, incinerated corpses of various insects line its cage.

Pets.Name: Bush Chicken
Pets.Description: Following their introduction to the mainland by the goblins, Bush Chickens have become the entre du jour for Pilgrim's Bounty due to their high deliciousness and low intelligence.

Pets.Name: Stonegrinder
Pets.Description: The master-crafted gear assembly and precision joints in this robot are clearly made with love.

Pets.Name: Lovebird Hatchling
Pets.Description: Of course there are hatchlings, they're called Lovebirds aren't they?

Pets.Name: Ore Eater
Pets.Description: They eat and eat and eat, until they grow large.  Then they eat some more.

Pets.Name: Syd the Squid
Pets.Description: This tiny squid seems to enjoy wearing pants, but where in the world would you find some that fit?

Pets.Name: Fruit Hunter
Pets.Description: This anxious avian has a voracious appetite for fruit.

Pets.Name: Sky-Bo
Pets.Description: The Sky-Bo was originally designed by the infamous Millie Watt as a toy for her cat.  The cat just slept in the box next to it.

Pets.Name: Bone Wasp
Pets.Description: One ogre ritual involves cupping a bone wasp in their hands and shaking vigorously.  The venom increases strength and invokes vivid imagery.

Pets.Name: River Calf
Pets.Description: Surprisingly quick when provoked, but easily calmed with a meal of Nagrand arrowbloom.

Pets.Name: Draenei Micro Defender
Pets.Description: While these tiny mechanical guardians were created to protect domesticated elekks from pests, they also turned out to be really good at giving back massages.

Pets.Name: Sea Calf
Pets.Description: The sea calf has a natural affinity for balancing things.

Pets.Name: Meadowstomper Calf
Pets.Description: The intelligence and caring nature of elekk calves make them a stalwart lifelong companion.

Pets.Name: Ghastly Kid
Pets.Description: In the ultimate demonstration of stubborness, this once-living nannie simply refused to die.

Pets.Name: Sun Sproutling
Pets.Description: From seed to flower, a tiny terror is grown.

Pets.Name: Nightshade Sproutling
Pets.Description: From seed to flower, a tiny terror is grown.

Pets.Name: Forest Sproutling
Pets.Description: From seed to flower, a tiny terror is grown.

Pets.Name: Autumnal Sproutling
Pets.Description: From seed to flower, a tiny terror is grown.

Pets.Name: Zomstrok
Pets.Description: Zomstrok is evil incarnate, the unholy mistake of someone maddened by isolation, a creation that could not be undone.

Pets.Name: Elekk Plushie
Pets.Description: This adorable little elephant is always there for you.

Pets.Name: Teroclaw Hatchling
Pets.Description: The teroclaw is a potent hunter of the small and furry.

Pets.Name: Lifelike Mechanical Frostboar
Pets.Description: After many complaints that taming young boars was a painful experience, engineers came to the rescue with this creepily lifelike model.

Pets.Name: Mechanical Axebeak
Pets.Description: Squeeeeeeeawk!

Pets.Name: Crazy Carrot
Pets.Description: Left to their own devices, carrots will simply lose their minds.

Pets.Name: Lil' Leftovers
Pets.Description: Cute enough to eat, but not recommended.

Pets.Name: Weebomination
Pets.Description: Made from bits and pieces of vanquished battle pets, dark magic, and just a pinch of love.

Pets.Name: Iron Starlette
Pets.Description: This massive weapon of destruction has been miniaturized for use in the garden.  Moles beware!

Pets.Name: Albino Chimaeraling
Pets.Description: Both heads are equally fearsome on the terrifying chimaera, each capable of its own unique breath attack.

Pets.Name: Macabre Marionette
Pets.Description: These possessed puppets are given a semblance of life by whimsical spirits of the departed.

Pets.Name: Rotten Little Helper
Pets.Description: Fed up with long hours, low pay, and entitled adventurers whining about sub-standard Winter Veil gifts, these nasty little creatures fled their magical workshops to join up with the Abominable Greench.

Pets.Name: Jadefire Spirit
Pets.Description: The Jadefire Spirit is thought to be a living embodiment of Yu'lon's own jade breath.

Pets.Name: Harmonious Porcupette
Pets.Description: Prolonged exposure to Shaohao's tranquil mists has calmed these spirits, which act as peaceful companions to the Mistweavers.

Pets.Name: Gu'chi Swarmling
Pets.Description: Swarmlings of Gu'chi spin an ancient, and very powerful, form of silk that has not been seen in Pandaria in ages.

Pets.Name: Vengeful Porcupette
Pets.Description: One of the beasts left on the Timeless Isle when it shifted through time, most likely sacrificed by the Ordon to their demigod, Ordos.

Pets.Name: Bonkers
Pets.Description: Some young and rambunctious hozen are trained from an early age to use boxing gloves so they don't accidentally kill their tribe members, and more importantly, to keep them from picking their noses.

Pets.Name: Gulp Froglet
Pets.Description: Gulp frogs of the Timeless Isle can feast upon prey several times larger than themselves by slowly digesting the victim externally with a toxic slime.

Pets.Name: Spineclaw Crab
Pets.Description: Spineclaw crabs of Pandaria have existed for thousands of years and remain one of the continent's oldest creatures.

Pets.Name: Skunky Alemental
Pets.Description: When the village of Pi'jiu was attacked by the Ordon Yaungol generations ago, the villagers bravely erected a keg in the center of the village. The following morning, after the celebration had died down, the still-drunken Yaungol blearily agreed to a peace treaty.

Pets.Name: Ominous Flame
Pets.Description: The odd blue flames found within the Cavern of Lost Spirits have led many unwary adventurers to their doom.

Pets.Name: Kovok
Pets.Description: The tiny terror of the Dread Wastes, Kovok prefers a steady diet of "brain food:" mushan brains, human brains, any brains really. This strange nutritional requirement may be tangentally related to his enormous head.

Pets.Name: Jademist Dancer
Pets.Description: The Jademist Dancers of the Timeless Isle have a curious attraction to the steam vents of the northwestern shore.

Pets.Name: Gooey Sha-ling
Pets.Description: Sha-lings reflect the emotional state of their handler, becoming agitated, depressed, fearful, or doubtful depending on the magnitude of negative thoughts around them.

Pets.Name: Droplet of Y'Shaarj
Pets.Description: A small remnant of the power of Y'shaarj that spawned the Sha, a single Drop of Y'shaarj can magnify emotional states for thousands of yards, causing entire towns to shut down in fear or doubt.

Pets.Name: Death Adder Hatchling
Pets.Description: Late in life, if the environmental conditions are correct, a Death Adder may shed its skin and undergo a transformation into an Imperial Python.

Pets.Name: Dandelion Frolicker
Pets.Description: While fascinated by flowers, the frolicker has also been known to dance with blades of grass and the occasional weed.

Pets.Name: Ruby Droplet
Pets.Description: The waters of Ruby Lake are colored a strange reddish hue, possibly due to the ruby crystals found deep within the mountains of the Timeless Isle.

Pets.Name: Ashleaf Spriteling
Pets.Description: The Ashleaf Sprites of the Timeless Isle tirelessly protect the vegetation of the island from the burning fury of Ordos and his minions.

Pets.Name: Blackfuse Bombling
Pets.Description: The first bombling created in the Underhold, Siegecrafter Blackfuse couldn't bear to see it destroyed, and kept it as a friendly, if explosive, pet.

Pets.Name: Azure Crane Chick
Pets.Description: Crane chicks of the Timeless Isle sometimes sing a curious song that calms the nerves and soothes the soul.

Pets.Name: Lil' Bling
Pets.Description: This handsome little robot is the spitting image of his "Dad", Blingtron 4000.  As a Blingtron's generosity circuits don't mature until they have been built to maximum specification, you shouldn't expect any free handouts from him.

Pets.Name: Moon Moon
Pets.Description: Moonfang's cubs grow into massive, slavering, black-furred killing machines...except for Moon Moon.

Pets.Name: Rascal-Bot
Pets.Description: This little guy seems to like to hang out at the fringes of cataclysmic events. There is no evidence to suggest he has anything to do with the cause of these events, though - he's probably just an observer. Or maybe he just gets lost on his trek to other places.

Pets.Name: Filthling
Pets.Description: Battle pet trainers are strongly encouraged to wash their hands for at least 30 minutes after playing with the filthling.

Pets.Name: Viscous Horror
Pets.Description: These malicious oozes are highly sensitive to Primordius' pheromone signature.

Pets.Name: Living Fluid
Pets.Description: The dark pools in Primordius' chamber laid stagnant after the fall of the mogu, and murky thoughts began to coalesce.

Pets.Name: Gahz'rooki
Pets.Description: The trolls have a long and storied history of summoning hydras. While he might not be the biggest or strongest hydra, Gahz'rooki will bite your fingers quite hard if you get too close.

Pets.Name: Phoenix Hawk Hatchling
Pets.Description: These brightly-colored dragonhawks carry Kael'thas' forces swiftly and unerringly through the nether storm surrounding the Eye.

Pets.Name: Lesser Voidcaller
Pets.Description: The lanterns carried by Voidcallers bridge the realms of the living and the dead, and often attract wayward ghosts and spirits.

Pets.Name: Pocket Reaver
Pets.Description: Sent through the Twisting Nether by the great Archimonde the Defiler to terrorize tiny civilizations with their medium-sized stomping noises.

Pets.Name: Coilfang Stalker
Pets.Description: It is unknown how Lady Vashj empowers these Fen Striders with their mind-bending powers; even at an early age this creature shows significant psychic ability.

Pets.Name: Tainted Waveling
Pets.Description: The spawn of Hydross, considered by the elemental community to be the "Earl of Puddles."

Pets.Name: Tideskipper
Pets.Description: An astounding find for the scientific community of Azeroth, who have long pondered, often in taverns, what a baby Sea Giant might look like.

Pets.Name: Fiendish Imp
Pets.Description: A minion of Terestian Illhoof, this tricky imp was left in Karazhan when the portal to the Twisting Nether closed behind it.

Pets.Name: Netherspace Abyssal
Pets.Description: Upon leaving Netherspace, Malchezaar's Abyssals become strangely distorted by Karazhan's magical protections, rendering them tiny, and much less likely to spontaneously erupt in flames.

Pets.Name: Menagerie Custodian
Pets.Description: Primary responsibilities include dusting Medivh's many magical artifacts, an activity which has claimed the life, sanity, or physical composition of the many caretakers preceding Moroes.

Pets.Name: Lil' Bad Wolf
Pets.Description: The oft-forgotten offspring of the Big Bad Wolf, found gorged and asleep in a basket of sweets.

Pets.Name: Zandalari Toenibbler
Pets.Description: These adorable beasts are both cuddly and huggable, provided you do not enjoy having a face.

Pets.Name: Zandalari Footslasher
Pets.Description: Even at this size, these raptors can eviscerate an entire cow in less than 10 seconds.

Pets.Name: Zandalari Anklerender
Pets.Description: These tiny minions of Zandalar are adept at stowing away within the cargo holds of the enormous Zandalari warships.

Pets.Name: Tiny White Carp
Pets.Description: These hardy fish can survive in a wide range of climates, from the snow capped mountains of Kun-Lai Summit to the temperate, salty waters of the oceans around Pandaria. Dedicated fishermen can sometimes befriend one.

Pets.Name: Tiny Green Carp
Pets.Description: It is believed that the green coloration and stripes on the tiny green carp serve as camouflage in the waters of the Jade Forest, Valley of the Four Winds and the Krasarang Wilds. Dedicated fishermen can sometimes befriend one.

Pets.Name: Tiny Blue Carp
Pets.Description: These little guys can be found in the waters of the Timeless Isle, or swimming alongside Jewel Danio or Redbelly Mandarin. Dedicated fishermen can sometimes befriend one.

Pets.Name: Tiny Red Carp
Pets.Description: The red scales on these fish are believed to be a warning to predators of the Townlong Steppes and the Dread Wastes that their flesh is toxic. Dedicated fishermen can sometimes befriend one.

Pets.Name: Direhorn Runt
Pets.Description: The Zandalari breed specific Direhorn species for their small size for use as mounts.

Pets.Name: Pierre
Pets.Description: Every cook's dream wrapped up in a perfect bundle of metal and bolts. Too bad what he cooks up isn't suitable for humanoid consumption. According to Jard's notes, he intends to make this little guy for his friend, Emily Cole.

Pets.Name: Ji-Kun Hatchling
Pets.Description: Deadly offspring of the Ancient Mother, Ji-Kun Hatchlings are considered sacred treasures in many cultures.

Pets.Name: Spawn of G'nathus
Pets.Description: Some Pandaren believe G'nathus to be an ancient Zandalari Loa left behind to guard the waters south of Lei Shen's island citadel.

Pets.Name: Pygmy Direhorn
Pets.Description: Don't pet the pointy end. Note: all of the ends are pointy.

Pets.Name: Mountain Panda
Pets.Description: Cousin to the Snowy Panda, Mountain Pandas reside in the lower regions of Kun-Lai Summit.

Pets.Name: Snowy Panda
Pets.Description: The white fur of the Snowy Panda allows it to hide in the snowbanks of Kun-Lai Summit.

Pets.Name: Sunfur Panda
Pets.Description: These cuddly rascals enjoy basking in the sun, sleeping in the sun, and napping in the sun.

Pets.Name: Spectral Porcupette
Pets.Description: Something about this creature looks familiar...

Pets.Name: Son of Animus
Pets.Description: Forged in the Throne of Thunder, these tiny creations are servants of the great Dark Animus.

Pets.Name: Zandalari Kneebiter
Pets.Description: This unique raptor was born on the island of Zandalar and transported here along with the rest of the Zandalari army.

Pets.Name: Sunreaver Micro-Sentry
Pets.Description: Loyal to the Sunreavers from creation, these pint-sized constructs can still pack a punch.

Pets.Name: Living Sandling
Pets.Description: Born of sand, these dangerous creatures can form and disperse in the blink of an eye.

Pets.Name: Gusting Grimoire
Pets.Description: A fel-tainted tome whose secrets are so terrible and bizarre that its pages have taken on an eerie semblance of life. Found on many a warlock's nightstand.

Pets.Name: Mini Mindslayer
Pets.Description: The bane of many an adventurer, to look into its eyes is to truly know madness.

Pets.Name: Anubisath Idol
Pets.Description: An anubisath idol from the Temple of Ahn'qiraj, infused with ancient qiraji magic.

Pets.Name: Viscidus Globule
Pets.Description: This small globule didn't make it back to Viscidus in time to re-form.

Pets.Name: Death Talon Whelpguard
Pets.Description: A runt of the Black Talon, left to guard the whelps.

Pets.Name: Chrominius
Pets.Description: Opponents that face this fierce chromatic monstrosity just hope it doesn't have Time Stop!

Pets.Name: Untamed Hatchling
Pets.Description: One of Razorgore's eggs that miraculously survived in Blackwing Lair.

Pets.Name: Ashstone Core
Pets.Description: The cooled core of Golemagg, it appears to retain some semi-sentient properties.

Pets.Name: Corefire Imp
Pets.Description: These tiny flame imps roam the Molten Core in large swarms and can replenish their numbers rapidly.

Pets.Name: Harbinger of Flame
Pets.Description: Few of these fiery harbingers remain following Ragnaros' defeat in the Firelands.

Pets.Name: Stitched Pup
Pets.Description: When Gluth died to a band of brave adventurers, these lil' guys were found in the corpse, gnawing on giant, undead bones.

Pets.Name: Fungal Abomination
Pets.Description: Loatheb's body is covered in small pods capable of spreading blighted fungal spores up to 1000 yards given proper wind conditions.

Pets.Name: Giant Bone Spider
Pets.Description: Found in a dusty egg clutch at the back of Maexxna's lair, these creatures are already undead when they hatch.

Pets.Name: Pandaren Earth Spirit
Pets.Description: The elements of Pandaria can be cute and playful, until their homeland is threatened.

Pets.Name: Pandaren Air Spirit
Pets.Description: These spirits represent the heart of Pandaria's elemental powers.

Pets.Name: Pandaren Fire Spirit
Pets.Description: Though it appears to be a vaguely-related cousin to the Cinder Kitten, the two creatures actually share no ancestry.

Pets.Name: Darkmoon Eye
Pets.Description: Don't even think about cheating now.

Pets.Name: Darkmoon Hatchling
Pets.Description: Most of Violet's numerous forest strider hatchlings are turned into Faire fare by Stamp Thunderhorn, though a few are raised as mounts or purchased by adventurers looking for a petite purple pet.

Pets.Name: Imperial Silkworm
Pets.Description: This plump red caterpillar is world-renowned for creating the finest silk in Azeroth.

Pets.Name: Imperial Moth
Pets.Description: The larval form of this moth is world-renowned for creating the finest silk in Azeroth.

Pets.Name: Pandaren Water Spirit
Pets.Description: Water spirits are especially mischievous, and are known to frequent the canals of the Stormstout Brewery.

Pets.Name: Darkmoon Rabbit
Pets.Description: This rabbit is much stronger than it looks.

Pets.Name: Fishy
Pets.Description: Many believe (incorrectly) that goldfish have no memories. Trainers who have worked with them know this is clearly not the case and have the bruises to prove it.

Pets.Name: Sapphire Cub
Pets.Description: While each cub is crafted in the same fashion, tiny imperfections in the gemstones create a unique personality when imbued with life.  They are, however, always playful and friendly.

Pets.Name: Jade Owl
Pets.Description: Jade is naturally rich with life and color, making it the perfect raw material for this gentle companion.  It is said the wind created by their wings can soothe the troubled mind.

Pets.Name: Mechanical Pandaren Dragonling
Pets.Description: Constructed from a seemingly impossible mass of gears, springs, wires, and a little something special, this dragonling embodies the spirit of Pandaria as seen through an engineer's goggles.

Pets.Name: Aqua Strider
Pets.Description: Water striders serve an important role in Jinyu culture, acting as a mount, means of sending communications, and even a family pet.

Pets.Name: Grinder
Pets.Description: Mogu sorcerers, keepers of the secret to bringing stone to life, prefer these stolid companions over more beastly familiars.

Pets.Name: Jade Crane Chick
Pets.Description: A graceful creature from the isle of Pandaria.

Pets.Name: Sand Scarab
Pets.Description: Easily mistaken for a common beetle, the sting of a sand scarab will quickly clear up the misconception should you be unlucky enough to be stung by one.

Pets.Name: Tiny Goldfish
Pets.Description: This bubble is way worse than a tank.

Pets.Name: Terrible Turnip
Pets.Description: And you thought turnips at the dinner table were bad.

Pets.Name: Gilnean Raven
Pets.Description: Quoth the raven: the last word you said. It gets old.

Pets.Name: Shore Crawler
Pets.Description: Only found on the shores of Kezan, this lovely sea creature can be a bit snarky.

Pets.Name: Sun Darter Hatchling
Pets.Description: This unique species of faerie dragon is not usually found on Azeroth.

Pets.Name: Porcupette
Pets.Description: Despite the name you should absolutely never, ever pet it.

Pets.Name: Eye of the Legion
Pets.Description: Always watching. Always judging.

Pets.Name: Darkmoon Cub
Pets.Description: This snuggly kitten loves to frolic, but sometimes it seems to be chasing things that aren't really there.

Pets.Name: Festival Lantern
Pets.Description: The light from each lantern honors the soul of an ancestor spirit. During the Lunar Festival, these ghostly elders pass along their wisdom to the current generation.

Pets.Name: Lunar Lantern
Pets.Description: These magical lanterns are acquired at the Lunar Festival, which celebrates the defeat of the Burning Legion by the free races of Azeroth.

Pets.Name: Sea Pony
Pets.Description: Despite its diminutive size, it is impossible to miss a sea pony cutting through the water.

Pets.Name: Darkmoon Zeppelin
Pets.Description: Lacking the mines and rockets of its predecessor, this kid-friendly faire prize is a new favorite among Azeroth's children.

Pets.Name: Darkmoon Tonk
Pets.Description: A favorite of children across Azeroth, this tough little toy can withstand even the most aggressive imagination.

Pets.Name: Lumpy
Pets.Description: A stowaway from Greatfather Winter's workshop, this tiny elemental found himself trapped in a present that was stolen by the Abominable Greench.

Pets.Name: Darkmoon Turtle
Pets.Description: This shelled reptile bravely protects the home on its back with an unwaveringly happy disposition.

Pets.Name: Gregarious Grell
Pets.Description: The grell are embodiments of the wild side of nature. Tame ones are very rare.

Pets.Name: Darkmoon Monkey
Pets.Description: The monkeys of Azeroth are infamous tricksters, and these beloved companions of the Darkmoon Faire have taken that innate cunning to a whole new level.

Pets.Name: Purple Puffer
Pets.Description: You might think this fish out of water would be a weak opponent... until you see the intensity that lies within its protective bubble.

Pets.Name: Feline Familiar
Pets.Description: Ride with the moon (and this furry, whiskered witch) in the dead of night!

Pets.Name: Crimson Lasher
Pets.Description: Native to the ashen soils of the Firelands, the lasher was transplanted to Azeroth with the help of the Guardians of Hyjal.

Pets.Name: Hyjal Bear Cub
Pets.Description: The cubs of Hyjal suffered much during the Cataclysm. On the bright side, the devastation also led to their newfound love of heights and daredevil stunts.

Pets.Name: Guardian Cub
Pets.Description: Still a little clumsy, this tiny winged lion is learning how to be the best mount it can be!

Pets.Name: Voodoo Figurine
Pets.Description: Voodoo figurines were often empowered by flasks of mojo, troll sweat, and the flesh of tribal enemies.

Pets.Name: Pterrordax Hatchling
Pets.Description: Pterrordax eggs are incubated underneath the fossils of their forebears, allowing the hatchlings to absorb their ancestors' ferocity and intelligence.

Pets.Name: Legs
Pets.Description: Wild marsh walkers are dangerously aggressive, but they're loyal and affectionate if you manage to tame them.

Pets.Name: Winterspring Cub
Pets.Description: Winterspring cubs are happiest when frolicking in freshly fallen snow.

Pets.Name: Nightsaber Cub
Pets.Description: Elune made nightsabers silver and black so they could meld into the shadows on a moonlit night.

Pets.Name: Landro's Lichling
Pets.Description: How the goblin Landro Longshot initially came into possession of the lichling remains a mystery, but he was all too eager to wager it in a game of cards.

Pets.Name: Elementium Geode
Pets.Description: Often confused by miners as a rare gem, the geode initially makes its presence known by emitting melodic reverberations.

Pets.Name: Magic Lamp
Pets.Description: Higitus figi- hockity pocki- abracadab- prestidig--oh, pinfeathers!

Pets.Name: Scooter the Snail
Pets.Description: You'll find him in his shell, exactly where a snail has to be!

Pets.Name: Mr. Grubbs
Pets.Description: The grubs of the Plaguelands are a side effect of necromantic corruption and the massive number of corpses there. Mr. Grubbs is from a particularly acrobatic breed.

Pets.Name: Landro's Lil' XT
Pets.Description: Always 'ready to play,' Landro's Lil' XT is almost indistinguishable from the original. Prone to tantrums and narcolepsy.

Pets.Name: Tiny Shale Spider
Pets.Description: The elemental shale spider is no true arachnid, but it has durability and ferocity that make it feared even in Deepholm.

Pets.Name: Fox Kit
Pets.Description: The fighting in Tol Barad has made it difficult to find these kits, and numerous would-be owners have decimated the fox population there as a result.

Pets.Name: Clockwork Gnome
Pets.Description: Thought to be an original construct of the titans, the clockwork gnome has some gears smaller than the eye can see.

Pets.Name: Rustberg Gull
Pets.Description: This bird is a salty veteran of the endless naval battles surrounding Tol Barad--a veteran whose grown plump from picking through the leftovers, that is.

Pets.Name: Enchanted Lantern
Pets.Description: Reduces the likelihood of being eaten by monsters when you enter pitch-black places.

Pets.Name: Fossilized Hatchling
Pets.Description: You stare at her, and she just stares right back. So clever; sometimes it seems as if she's figuring out how to open doors.

Pets.Name: Crawling Claw
Pets.Description: This appears to be nothing more than the paw of a small primate. A closer inspection shows that the hand has been dried by centuries of exposure to the sands of Uldum.

Pets.Name: De-Weaponized Mechanical Companion
Pets.Description: The gold standard for sturdy gnomish design, this mechanical critter is the perfect companion for the lonely engineer on the go.

Pets.Name: Personal World Destroyer
Pets.Description: Goblin engineering at its finest. Blueprints based on the massive fel reaver in Hellfire Peninsula.

Pets.Name: Blue Clockwork Rocket Bot
Pets.Description: Jepetto's top-selling toy this season, the bot's new blue chassis has children across Azeroth scrambling for this feisty battle bot.

Pets.Name: Frigid Frostling
Pets.Description: While not nearly as destructive as its original master, the Ice Lord Ahune, the frostling has been known to lob chilly snowballs at unsuspecting adventurers.

Pets.Name: Toxic Wasteling
Pets.Description: This, um, 'adorable' little guy likes to play with critters. Just don't let them get too close.

Pets.Name: Spectral Tiger Cub
Pets.Description: Affectionate and curious, these kittens seem to find the most peculiar hiding places.

Pets.Name: Tuskarr Kite
Pets.Description: Seeing these kites soaring above Northrend's shores is a clear sign that tuskarr are nearby.

Pets.Name: Razzashi Hatchling
Pets.Description: Razzashi raptors are among the cleverest predators in the jungle, making them favored by the Gurubashi trolls.

Pets.Name: Razormaw Hatchling
Pets.Description: Razormaw eggs are protected fiercely by their mothers. Their cuteness belies their inherited ferocity.

Pets.Name: Ravasaur Hatchling
Pets.Description: Larger than other raptor infants, these hatchlings grow into towering reptilian beasts capable of massive devastation.

Pets.Name: Obsidian Hatchling
Pets.Description: This hatchling is coveted for its unique coloration. Despite Breanni's loving care, the raptor's feral nature cannot be restrained.

Pets.Name: Leaping Hatchling
Pets.Description: This offspring of Takk the Leaper is incredibly agile and can maneuver through difficult environments with ease.

Pets.Name: Gundrak Hatchling
Pets.Description: This crafty raptor was born in the icy wastes of Northrend. Its harsh upbringing gives it strength when many others would perish.

Pets.Name: Deviate Hatchling
Pets.Description: The demand for this purple-skinned breed has drastically reduced the raptor population near the Wailing Caverns.

Pets.Name: Darting Hatchling
Pets.Description: Rarely leaves nest until maturity. Enjoys conducting pranks on the nearby Razormaw nest.

Pets.Name: Shimmering Wyrmling
Pets.Description: This extremely rare species of mana wyrm is prized for its unique coloration and even temperament.

Pets.Name: Calico Cat
Pets.Description: Many have mocked this cat before battle. Their last words are usually, 'Here, kitty, kitty.'

Pets.Name: Sen'jin Fetish
Pets.Description: This is actually three pets in one. You won't know what you'll get until you summon it!

Pets.Name: Mechanopeep
Pets.Description: Used as a training mount for young gnomes, the Mechanopeep has none of the speed but all of the charm of its bigger brothers.

Pets.Name: Enchanted Broom
Pets.Description: Created by a blood elf apprentice, this broom would be extremely useful if anyone could figure out how to make it stop.

Pets.Name: Ammen Vale Lashling
Pets.Description: The long-term effects of the contamination caused by the Exodar are still under observation.

Pets.Name: Strand Crawler
Pets.Description: The strand crawler has adapted to the northern cold by hibernating under the island beaches.

Pets.Name: Mulgore Hatchling
Pets.Description: A tallstrider's speed on open ground is unrivaled, so predators rely on stealth and pack tactics when hunting them.

Pets.Name: Elwynn Lamb
Pets.Description: Unlike the polymorphed variety, trueborn sheep can be quite scary.

Pets.Name: Durotar Scorpion
Pets.Description: The volatile Durotar scorpion is a fierce desert predator. It's known for only occasionally stinging its owner.

Pets.Name: Tirisfal Batling
Pets.Description: Can sense an opponent's heartbeat through echolocation.

Pets.Name: Dun Morogh Cub
Pets.Description: The bears of Dun Morogh are prized for their hardiness, loyalty, and combat prowess. Their time among the dwarves does little to curb their ferocity.

Pets.Name: Teldrassil Sproutling
Pets.Description: It always wakes up dancing. What a happy little tree!

Pets.Name: Spring Rabbit
Pets.Description: Spring rabbits symbolize fertility, and they love to demonstrate why.

Pets.Name: Pengu
Pets.Description: Notable for its wide eyes and sassy slide.

Pets.Name: Cobra Hatchling
Pets.Description: The cobras of Azeroth are renowned for their fast-acting venom and swift strikes.

Pets.Name: Proto-Drake Whelp
Pets.Description: These voracious whelps grow at an incredible rate and can devour nearly twice their weight in raw meat per day.

Pets.Name: White Tickbird Hatchling
Pets.Description: The rare albino tickbird is revered amongst the Oracles. It is seen as a sign of heavy rainstorms, good grub harvests, and hibernating wolvar.

Pets.Name: Tickbird Hatchling
Pets.Description: Unlike most of Geen the gorloc's 'mysterious eggs,' this one actually contained a living thing.

Pets.Name: Giant Sewer Rat
Pets.Description: You wouldn't want to meet this rat in a dark alley. Or anywhere else, for that matter.

Pets.Name: Clockwork Rocket Bot
Pets.Description: This mechanical warrior is a favorite gift during Winter Veil. Batteries not included, but rockets definitely are!

Pets.Name: Ghostly Skull
Pets.Description: When it senses the impending death of a nearby creature, the skull will emit an ear-piercing cackle.

Pets.Name: Nether Ray Fry
Pets.Description: Native to the Terokkar Forest, nether rays spawn during the rainy season. Out of the hundreds of offspring resulting from one hatching, very few survive to adulthood.

Pets.Name: Ethereal Soul-Trader
Pets.Description: Ethereal soul-traders will travel with those who can free the essences they need from unruly hosts.

Pets.Name: Phoenix Hatchling
Pets.Description: A child of phoenix god Al'ar, the hatchling takes flight immediately after birth.

Pets.Name: Chuck
Pets.Description: After studying the crocolisk species for several years, the famed angler Nat Pagle determined that baby crocolisks are mostly harmless.

Pets.Name: Snarly
Pets.Description: Crocolisks are six-legged amphibian predators found in coastal waters. Archaeologists believe they may be descended from the diemetradons of Un'Goro Crater.

Pets.Name: Searing Scorchling
Pets.Description: Elder shaman have warned that wisps of Ragnaros, loyal servants of the Firelord, have taken to the Molten Front. Vigilance is advised.

Pets.Name: Dragon Kite
Pets.Description: Much quieter than a real dragon.

Pets.Name: Rocket Chicken
Pets.Description: When the Burning Legion invaded Outland, Landro Longshot contributed this design to the war effort. Both the Alliance and Horde passed on production.

Pets.Name: Tiny Sporebat
Pets.Description: Wild sporebats are vicious creatures, but if tamed at a young age, they can be loyal companions.

Pets.Name: Pint-Sized Pink Pachyderm
Pets.Description: A shy creature, the pink elekk will only make its presence known to those that have befriended it, or to anyone too intoxicated to harm it.

Pets.Name: Mojo
Pets.Description: Amani witch doctors have turned many victims into frogs. Some never recover.

Pets.Name: Muckbreath
Pets.Description: Muckbreath is just like other crocolisks, except he has an excellent sense of humor.

Pets.Name: Toothy
Pets.Description: The crocolisk's hide and meat are prized by hunters, but the beast is ferocious and seldom taken by surprise.

Pets.Name: Sinister Squashling
Pets.Description: This mischievous gourd likes to pop out of the ground and scare you right out of your pants. Hail to the pumpkin song!

Pets.Name: Peanut
Pets.Description: Domesticated elekks are trained as mounts by the draenei people. In the wild they are hunted for their ivory tusks.

Pets.Name: Egbert
Pets.Description: The sin'dorei use hawkstriders as mounts, but this little one was deemed unfit after being diagnosed with numerous anxiety disorders.

Pets.Name: Willy
Pets.Description: He's slightly evil but extremely narcoleptic. Keep him away from critters!

Pets.Name: Bananas
Pets.Description: Although loath to part with Bananas, Landro realized it would be safer if the pet was far from Booty Bay when his father, King Mukla, discovered he was missing.

Pets.Name: Wolpertinger
Pets.Description: Many a drunken Brewfest goer has told the tale of this mysterious creature, but few remember that tale in the morning.

Pets.Name: Firefly
Pets.Description: Commonly used by sporelings as a light source as they navigate the waterways of Zangarmarsh, this luminescent insect helps them avoid the dangerous bogflare needlers.

Pets.Name: Blue Dragonhawk Hatchling
Pets.Description: Raised by the few dealers still willing to trade in Netherstorm.

Pets.Name: Silver Dragonhawk Hatchling
Pets.Description: Ferocious species of dragonhawk, often seen diving down on prey from high in the sky.

Pets.Name: Red Dragonhawk Hatchling
Pets.Description: Mortal enemies with silver dragonhawks; on pretty good terms with golden dragonhawks.

Pets.Name: Golden Dragonhawk Hatchling
Pets.Description: Once prized by dragonhawk breeders in Silvermoon. Stands out at night to predators, making it necessary to gain awareness at an early age.

Pets.Name: White Moth
Pets.Description: Prefers flying during foggy nights, which are common in Azuremyst Isle.

Pets.Name: Yellow Moth
Pets.Description: Prefers flying during thunderstorms, which are common in Azuremyst Isle.

Pets.Name: Red Moth
Pets.Description: A product of exposure to wild magical energies, this insect is the unexpectedly colorful result of Azerothian moth species being released into the eco-domes of the Netherstorm.

Pets.Name: Blue Moth
Pets.Description: Delicate denizen of Azuremyst Isle, this magical insect has been rumored to possess healing abilities.

Pets.Name: Brown Rabbit
Pets.Description: This bunny is the cutest thing its opponent will ever see. It'll also be the last.

Pets.Name: Mana Wyrmling
Pets.Description: The ethereal Rashaad began breeding these enigmatic creatures after learning of their ability to feed on arcane energies.

Pets.Name: Magical Crawdad
Pets.Description: Only magical wishes make magical crawdads. If you wish for a fish, fish for a wish.

Pets.Name: Hippogryph Hatchling
Pets.Description: After a harpy incursion in Feralas, several hippogryph nesting grounds were attacked. Hundreds of hatchlings were orphaned, and they have been entrusted to the care of the druids on Sardor Isle.

Pets.Name: Spirit of Summer
Pets.Description: This little piece of summer smells of warm evenings and lightning storms. If you look closely, you might be able to make out a face.

Pets.Name: Whiskers the Rat
Pets.Description: A clever tactician, Whiskers will feign death to fool predators and bill collectors.

Pets.Name: Mr. Wiggles
Pets.Description: Many assume 'Wiggles' refers to the motion of this pig's posterior. They discover the true meaning when they see their own entrails.

Pets.Name: Speedy
Pets.Description: Slow and steady wins the race.

Pets.Name: Peddlefeet
Pets.Description: The power of love can be a dangerous thing, especially when wielded by an airborne, ill-tempered goblin.

Pets.Name: Winter's Little Helper
Pets.Description: During the majestic Winter Veil celebration, these faithful companions spread joy throughout the land. The rest of the year, they are busy.

Pets.Name: Father Winter's Helper
Pets.Description: These off-season allies work tirelessly to prepare for the Feast of Winter Veil, and would appreciate not being disturbed the rest of the year.

Pets.Name: Winter Reindeer
Pets.Description: Would rather not spend any more time inside a wrapped holiday gift box.

Pets.Name: Tiny Snowman
Pets.Description: He loves to dance and wave and play with you! He melts after Winter Veil, but don't worry; he'll be back again someday!

Pets.Name: Tranquil Mechanical Yeti
Pets.Description: This clever toy is equipped with a secret switch to disable its 'tranquil' mode. Luckily, even the engineers who built it don't know how to find it.

Pets.Name: Land Shark
Pets.Description: After birth, newborn sharks quickly flee their hungry mothers. Their early independence makes them dangerous, even while young.

Pets.Name: Disgusting Oozeling
Pets.Description: Warning: Wear gloves and goggles while handling disgusting oozeling. DO NOT WASH. Will stain most armor.

Pets.Name: Lifelike Toad
Pets.Description: Talented engineers have crafted an exact replica of a wood frog, at only ten times the cost of buying the real thing.

Pets.Name: Smolderweb Hatchling
Pets.Description: Although born in the warm heights of Blackrock Spire, the smolderweb hatchling can survive most environments.

Pets.Name: Worg Pup
Pets.Description: Worgs are the favored companions of orcs and are fiercely loyal on the battlefield.

Pets.Name: Sprite Darter Hatchling
Pets.Description: These adorable critters love snuggling with their owners after a long day of brutal, bloody battle.

Pets.Name: Lil' Smoky
Pets.Description: This tiny peacekeeper security bot is often outfitted with the latest arcane nullifiers and crowd pummelers. Not for the environmentally minded.

Pets.Name: Pet Bombling
Pets.Description: Possibly explosive, definitely adorable. Keep away from open flame.

Pets.Name: Crimson Snake
Pets.Description: The crimson snake is favored among the Darkspear trolls for not only its vicious nature, but also its steadfast loyalty.

Pets.Name: Brown Snake
Pets.Description: The brown snake is native to Horde-controlled territories. It seems unremarkable until its victims are within range.

Pets.Name: Black Kingsnake
Pets.Description: Xan'tish fearlessly tracks, captures, and trains these noble snakes so they will aid their comrades in battle.

Pets.Name: Albino Snake
Pets.Description: A favored companion of Kirin Tor magi, this reptile makes one wonder whether it's truly a snake or something else altogether.

Pets.Name: Snowshoe Rabbit
Pets.Description: If you go chasing snowshoe rabbits, you know you're going to fall.

Pets.Name: Brown Prairie Dog
Pets.Description: A complex system of burrows protects the prairie dog from its neighbors' massive hooves.

Pets.Name: Great Horned Owl
Pets.Description: Night elf children are given an owl feather on their first birthday as a token of good luck.

Pets.Name: Hawk Owl
Pets.Description: This dark-feathered bird of prey is often seen as a harbinger of doom amongst the druids of Teldrassil.

Pets.Name: Tree Frog
Pets.Description: Known for their powerful legs and keen eyesight.

Pets.Name: Wood Frog
Pets.Description: Vendors at the Darkmoon Faire offer strange and exotic wonders. They also sell wood frogs.

Pets.Name: Emerald Whelpling
Pets.Description: Once bred and raised by the green dragon Itharius, this unique breed of dragon has settled in the jungles of Feralas.

Pets.Name: Crimson Whelpling
Pets.Description: Hailing from the Wetlands, this young dragon is just learning how to hunt, breathe fire, and go on cute destructive rampages.

Pets.Name: Azure Whelpling
Pets.Description: Like other members of the blue dragonflight, these whimsical little critters display an affinity to arcane magic.

Pets.Name: Dark Whelpling
Pets.Description: Hopes that someday it can grow up to destroy villages, just like mommy.

Pets.Name: Undercity Cockroach
Pets.Description: The cockroach is usually an impulse buy. Its owners have a hard time parting with it even after numerous attempts.

Pets.Name: Ancona Chicken
Pets.Description: It thinks you taste like chicken too.

Pets.Name: Senegal
Pets.Description: Favored pet of the goblins of Booty Bay, this colorful bird is renowned for its ability to count coins, tally budgets, and lie about contracts.

Pets.Name: Green Wing Macaw
Pets.Description: Favored pet of the Defias pirates, this colorful bird is handy for remembering passwords, grocery lists, and cracker recipes.

Pets.Name: Hyacinth Macaw
Pets.Description: The jungle trolls train these birds to mimic calls for help in order to lure unsuspecting travelers into traps.

Pets.Name: Cockatiel
Pets.Description: Some say this clever bird can be taught to speak--but it's smart enough to keep its beak shut.

Pets.Name: White Kitten
Pets.Description: 'The queen had three kittens. The first gave her a juicy rat. The second, a tasty hare. And the white, her favorite, presented the head of her rival.'

Pets.Name: Silver Tabby Cat
Pets.Description: Sleeping is this cat's second favorite activity. The first is yawning.

Pets.Name: Siamese Cat
Pets.Description: Known for its blue eyes. Also considered a delicacy by giant murlocs.

Pets.Name: Orange Tabby Cat
Pets.Description: The last person who tried to housebreak this cat quickly learned that a soiled rug is better than a shredded everything-else.

Pets.Name: Black Tabby Cat
Pets.Description: Old Alterac saying: 'A cat has nine lives, but needs only one.'

Pets.Name: Cornish Rex Cat
Pets.Description: Donni Anthania invites these cats to her tea parties. But she doesn't serve their favorite drink: the tears of their enemies.

Pets.Name: Bombay Cat
Pets.Description: Donni Anthania plans to have a bombay buried with her when she dies. A wise adventurer can put its talents to much better use.

Pets.Name: Mechanical Squirrel
Pets.Description: A mechanical squirrel's logic center tells it to collect and store both nuts and bolts for the winter.
